Relocation expenses will <u data-stringify-type="underline">not</u>&nbsp;be provided for this role. &nbsp;&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><strong>You should apply if:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>You want to impact the industries that run our world: </strong>Your efforts will result in real-world impact—helping to keep the lights on, get food into grocery stores, and most importantly, ensure workers return home safely.</li> <li><strong>You have an innate curiosity about how businesses work: </strong>One day you’ll meet with someone in waste management and the next you may be learning about the inner workings of a food distribution center. Our top sales team members seek to learn the ins and outs of the businesses they support in order to make a larger impact.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>You build genuine relationships with your customers:</strong> The industries we serve have relied on pen-and-paper solutions for years and haven’t been met with the type of technology we offer. Our customer's value earned trust and human relationships built over time.</li> <li><strong>You want to be with the best:</strong> Samsara’s high-performance culture means you’ll be surrounded by the best and challenged to go farther than you have before.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>You are a team player:</strong> At Samsara, sales is a team sport. We help each other out by sharing best practices and focusing on winning as a team.</li> </ul> <p><strong>In this role, you will:</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Champion, role model, and embed Samsara’s cultural principles (Focus on Customer Success, Build for the Long Term, Adopt a Growth Mindset, Be Inclusive, Win as a Team) as we scale globally and across new offices</li> <li>Become an expert on Samsara products: where the rubber of cutting-edge tech (IoT, AI, computer vision) meets the road of driving digital transformation for physical operations.</li> <li>Articulate to prospects how businesses like theirs use Samsara today to solve challenges common to their industry.</li> <li>Deliver the work products that get deals done.&nbsp; Think discoveries, demonstrations, value assessments, proof of concept implementations, etc.</li> <li>Walk installers through any scenario, explaining best practices for installing Samsara products and helping build deployment plans.</li> <li>Write scripts against our open API that solve real-world problems for companies that deliver essential services (for example), and document them for your fellow SE’s to use.</li> <li>Open the door for deeper entrenchment into accounts (integration, upsell, cross sell) by becoming a consultant to your customers.</li> <li>Act as a liaison between Samsara’s product and sales teams, translating technical feedback and enrolling the right customers in our beta programs.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Minimum requirements for the role:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Bachelors’ of Science degree from a 4 year accredited university&nbsp;</li> <li>2+ years experience working with customers in a pre-sales, customer success, or customer/product support capacity</li> <li>Ability to influence stakeholders based on your technical sales acumen and a solid understanding of business processes and change management</li> <li>Ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences</li> <li>Previous experience managing hardware and/or software product evaluations</li> <li>Basic understanding of electronics and electrical systems</li> <li>Experience with the following concepts: technical consulting, cloud software, cloud-connected hardware, computer networking, business systems integration, automation and control systems</li> <li>Must be located within and authorized to work in the United States</li> </ul> <p><strong>An ideal candidate also has:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Bachelors’ of Science degree or advanced education in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, or related discipline</li> <li>Work experience in task automation scripting and transferring data between systems using Bash, Python, JavaScript, or equivalent language</li> <li>Work experience building solutions leveraging open API’s</li> <li>Understanding of vehicle diagnostic systems, including Controller Area Networks (CAN bus) and Power Take Off (PTO) applications</li> <li>Hobbyist interest in working with things driven by electronic systems (car audio installation/classic car restoration, home automation, building things with raspberry pi, arduino, etc.)</li> <li>Consistent track record of success selling complex technical solutions through strategic sales cycles</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><span style="font-size: 16px;">The range of annual on-target earnings (OTE) range for full-time employees for this position is below. Please note that OTE pay may vary depending on factors including your city of residence, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Learn more about our total rewards and benefits below.</span></p></div><div class="title">Annual OTE Salary</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$95,795</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$144,900 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Total Rewards</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we build for the people who keep the global economy moving. We want owners, not passengers, which is why <a href="https://www.samsara.com/company/careers/benefits">our rewards</a> are designed to fuel high-impact builders. Our compensation program delivers above-market total compensation through a combination of base salary, performance-based bonus/variable pay, and equity (for eligible roles) in a high-growth public company. We meaningfully differentiate pay for our top performers, who have the opportunity to earn above-market compensation that can outpace the broader market over time.</p> <p>Beyond compensation, we provide the foundations that enable long-term success: a flexible, employee-led remote model, a professional development stipend, comprehensive health and parental leave plans, and more. If you’re ready to build for the long term and own the outcome, your journey starts here.</p> <p><strong>Flexible Working&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Samsara, we embrace a flexible working model that caters to the diverse needs of our teams. Our offices are open for those who prefer to work in-person and we also support remote work where it aligns with our operational requirements. For certain positions, being close to one of our offices or within a specific geographic area is important to facilitate collaboration, access to resources, or alignment with our service regions. In these cases, the job description will clearly indicate any working location requirements. Our goal is to ensure that all members of our team can contribute effectively, whether they are working on-site, in a hybrid model, or fully remotely. All offers of employment are contingent upon an individual’s ability to secure and maintain the legal right to work at the company and in the specified work location, if applicable.</span></p> <p><strong>Belonging at Samsara</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we welcome everyone regardless of their background. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, and other characteristics protected by law. We depend on the unique approaches of our team members to help us solve complex problems and want to ensure that Samsara is a place where people from all backgrounds can make an impact.</p> <p><strong>Accommodations&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Samsara is an inclusive work environment, and we are committed to ensuring equal opportunity in employment for qualified persons with disabilities. Please email <a href="mailto:accessibleinterviewing@samsara.com">accessibleinterviewing@samsara.com</a> or <a href="https://forms.gle/DrM6DFQUiSgfPtCVA">click here</a> if you require any reasonable accommodations throughout the recruiting process.</p> <p><strong>Our Commitment to Authenticity</strong></p> <p>We use <a href="https://hiretofu.com/privacyPolicy">Tofu</a>, a fraud detection tool, to validate the authenticity of applications and protect against identity fraud. This ensures we are connecting with real people and allows us to prioritize genuine candidates. Please see Samsara’s <a href="https://www.samsara.com/legal/candidate-privacy-notice">Candidate Privacy Notice</a> for more information.</p> <p><strong>Fraudulent Employment Offers</strong></p> <p>Samsara is aware of scams involving fake job interviews and offers. Please know we do not charge fees to applicants at any stage of the hiring process. Official communication about your application will only come from emails ending in @samsara.com, @us-greenhouse-mail.io or @mail3.guide.co. For more information regarding fraudulent employment offers, please visit our&nbsp;<a href="https://samsara.com/blog/avoiding-fake-job-offers">blog post here</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Backend Engineer, Analytics Instrumentation (Golang)
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>GitLab is the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps. GitLab enables organizations to increase developer productivity, improve operational efficiency, reduce security and compliance risk, and accelerate digital transformation. More than 50 million registered users and more than 50% of the Fortune 100* trust GitLab to ship better, more secure software faster.</p> <p>The same principles built into our products are reflected in how our team works: we embrace AI as a core productivity multiplier, with all team members expected to incorporate AI into their daily workflows to drive efficiency, innovation, and impact. GitLab is where careers accelerate, innovation flourishes, and every voice is valued. Our high-performance culture is driven by our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/values/">values</a> and continuous knowledge exchange, enabling our team members to reach their full potential while collaborating with industry leaders to solve complex problems. <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OuZIb5zszQI">Co-create the future with us</a> as we build technology that transforms how the world develops software.</p> <p>*<em>Fortune 500® is a registered trademark of Fortune Media IP Limited, used under license. Claim based on GitLab data. Fortune 100 refers to the top 20% ranked companies in the 2025 Fortune 500 list, published in June 2025. Fortune and Fortune Media IP Limited are not affiliated with, and do not endorse products or services of GitLab.</em></p></div><p>An overview of this role</p> <p>As an Intermediate Backend Engineer, you'll guide the design and development of backend systems that help the company identify customer usage patterns across GitLab SaaS and Self-Managed deployments. That data informs product decisions. This role offers the opportunity to build foundational infrastructure that makes instrumentation simpler and more reliable for teams at GitLab.</p> <h2>What you’ll do</h2> <ul> <li value="1">Design, build, and maintain a unified Go-based instrumentation service that consolidates instrumentation across the entire company, eliminating the need for multiple language-specific SDKs while maintaining reliability and performance.</li> <li value="2">Manage the handling of the sending, transit, and quality of instrumentation data across the system, ensuring data integrity that directly impacts the company's key prioritization and usage billing accuracy.</li> <li value="3">Train and assist product development teams across the company on how to instrument their features using the unified service, providing documentation, guidance, and technical help.</li> <li value="4">Manage on-call duties during working hours for systems that handle usage billing and instrumentation, ensuring system reliability and quick response to critical issues.</li> <li value="5">Work across research and development teams and the enterprise data organization to identify requirements and deliver solutions that serve multiple stakeholders.</li> <li value="6">Make key architectural decisions that balance the needs of product teams (who need ease of use) with data consumers (who need reliability and correctness), ensuring the system serves as a foundational service for the company.</li> </ul> <h2>What you’ll bring</h2> <ul> <li value="1">Proficiency in the Go programming language, with experience building and maintaining production services.</li> <li value="2">Strong backend development experience, with the ability to design scalable, reliable systems serving internal and external customers.</li> <li value="3">Experience with infrastructure concerns such as system reliability, performance at scale, data quality, and observability.</li> <li value="4">Experience designing and building APIs (REST, gRPC, or similar) that other teams integrate with.</li> <li value="5">Experience working in cross-functional teams with product teams, data consumers, and other internal stakeholders across team boundaries.</li> <li value="6">Experience with instrumentation, analytics, data systems, or similar foundational infrastructure in application environments such as Ruby on Rails or comparable stacks.</li> </ul> <h2>About the team</h2> <p>Our team is part of the Data Engineering organization and runs a foundational service used by all research and development teams at GitLab. We manage the systems that send, transport, and validate instrumentation data across the company, giving us visibility into customer usage patterns across GitLab SaaS and Self-Managed deployment environments. This data informs usage billing and product planning. We're building a unified, Go-based instrumentation service that replaces multiple language-specific SDKs, making it easier for teams to instrument their features while ensuring data integrity for billing and analysis. As a distributed team working asynchronously across multiple time zones, we work closely with product and engineering teams across the company and provide guidance on instrumenting features effectively.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h4><strong>How GitLab Supports Full-Time Employees</strong></h4> <ul> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/">Benefits to support your health, finances, and well-being</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/paid-time-off/">Flexible Paid Time Off</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/erg-guide/#:~:text=Definition%20of%20the%20TMRG%20%2D%20Team%20Member%20Resource%20Groups,-TMRGs%20are%20voluntary&amp;text=The%20purpose%20for%20this%20type,developing%20the%20sense%20of%20belonging.">Team Member Resource Groups</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/stock-options/">Equity Compensation &amp; Employee Stock Purchase Plan</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/learning-and-development/growth-and-development/">Growth and Development Fund</a></li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/#parental-leave">Parental leave</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/finance/expenses/#equipment">Home office</a> support</li> </ul> <p>Please note that we welcome interest from candidates with varying levels of experience; many successful candidates do not meet every single requirement. Additionally, studies have shown that people from <a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/#examples-of-select-underrepresented-groups">underrepresented groups</a> are less likely to apply to a job unless they meet every single qualification. If you're excited about this role, please apply and allow our recruiters to assess your application.</p> <hr> <p><strong>Country Hiring Guidelines: </strong>GitLab hires new team members in countries around the world. All of our roles are remote, however some roles may carry specific location-based eligibility requirements. Our Talent Acquisition team can help answer any questions about location after starting the recruiting process.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Privacy Policy: </strong>Please review our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/candidate-faq/recruitment-privacy-policy/" target="_blank">Recruitment Privacy Policy.</a> Your privacy is important to us.</p> <p>GitLab is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. GitLab’s policies and practices relating to recruitment, employment, career development and advancement, promotion, and retirement are based solely on merit, regardless of race, color, religion, ancestry, sex (including pregnancy, lactation, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression), national origin, age, citizenship, marital status, mental or physical disability, genetic information (including family medical history), discharge status from the military, protected veteran status (which includes disabled veterans, recently separated veterans, active duty wartime or campaign badge veterans, and Armed Forces service medal veterans), or any other basis protected by law. GitLab will not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on any of these characteristics. See also&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/people-policies/inc-usa/#equal-employment-opportunity-policy" target="_blank">GitLab’s EEO Policy</a>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/labor-and-employment-notices/#eeoc-us-equal-employment-opportunity-commission-notices" target="_blank">EEO is the Law</a>. If you have a disability or special need that requires&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/people-policies/inc-usa/#reasonable-accommodation" target="_blank">accommodation</a>, please let us know during the&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/interviewing/#adjustments-to-our-interview-process" target="_blank">recruiting process</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Engineering Manager, AI Engineering:Workflow Catalog
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>GitLab is the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps. GitLab enables organizations to increase developer productivity, improve operational efficiency, reduce security and compliance risk, and accelerate digital transformation. More than 50 million registered users and more than 50% of the Fortune 100* trust GitLab to ship better, more secure software faster.</p> <p>The same principles built into our products are reflected in how our team works: we embrace AI as a core productivity multiplier, with all team members expected to incorporate AI into their daily workflows to drive efficiency, innovation, and impact. GitLab is where careers accelerate, innovation flourishes, and every voice is valued. Our high-performance culture is driven by our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/values/">values</a> and continuous knowledge exchange, enabling our team members to reach their full potential while collaborating with industry leaders to solve complex problems. <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OuZIb5zszQI">Co-create the future with us</a> as we build technology that transforms how the world develops software.</p> <p>*<em>Fortune 500® is a registered trademark of Fortune Media IP Limited, used under license. Claim based on GitLab data. Fortune 100 refers to the top 20% ranked companies in the 2025 Fortune 500 list, published in June 2025. Fortune and Fortune Media IP Limited are not affiliated with, and do not endorse products or services of GitLab.</em></p></div><h2>An overview of this role</h2> <p>As an <span class="textBold">Engineering Manager, <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/engineering/ai/workflow-catalog/">AI Engineering: Workflow Catalog</a></span>, you will guide the team building <a href="https://gitlab.com/explore/ai-catalog/agents">GitLab’s <span class="textBold">AI catalog</span></a>, which helps customers and internal teams create, share, version, and govern AI agents and workflows across GitLab. This role sits at the center of GitLab’s work on <span class="textBold">AI</span> and the <span class="textBold">Duo Agent Platform</span>, with responsibility for helping a team of engineers deliver systems that let AI agents run at scale with the right controls in place. You will report through engineering leadership while managing a team of 9 engineers, and your work in the first year will focus on giving the team clarity, improving execution, and shipping customer-facing capabilities that make AI agents easier to build, manage, and trust.</p> <p>This is a hands-on management opportunity for someone who wants to have impact through both people management and technical judgment. You’ll help shape how software teams use AI in GitLab, stay close to technical design and day-to-day development, and partner closely with product and UX to turn complex ideas into practical features.</p> <h2>What you’ll do</h2> <ul> <li value="1"><span class="textBold">Guide</span> a distributed team of frontend, backend, and AI engineers working on the Workflow Catalog to deliver roadmap commitments on schedule.</li> <li value="2"><span class="textBold">Coach</span> engineers through feedback, enablement, and clear expectations to improve performance, growth, and retention across the team.</li> <li value="3"><span class="textBold">Work </span><span class="textBold">with</span> product and UX counterparts to define requirements and deliver AI features that address customer needs and improve adoption.</li> <li value="4"><span class="textBold">Participate</span> in technical design discussions and contribute engineering judgment to build scalable, reliable systems that support AI agents running at scale.</li> <li value="5"><span class="textBold">Remove</span> blockers, create clarity, and help the team maintain steady delivery in a fast-moving environment.</li> <li value="6"><span class="textBold">Coordinate</span> execution across planning, development, and delivery to improve predictability, delivery speed, and quality.</li> <li value="7"><span class="textBold">Promote</span> a team culture grounded in inclusion, curiosity, technical craft, and continuous delivery to strengthen collaboration and engineering excellence.</li> <li value="8"><span class="textBold">Work **with</span> teams across GitLab’s AI space to enable the broader Duo Agent Platform and ship aligned AI capabilities across the product.</li> </ul> <h2>What you’ll bring</h2> <ul> <li value="1"><span class="textBold">Experience managing</span> effective engineering teams and assisting engineers with coaching, feedback, and career development.</li> <li value="2"><span class="textBold">Solid technical background</span> in software development, with the ability to engage deeply in architecture and engineering judgment.</li> <li value="3"><span class="textBold">Hands-on familiarity</span> with agentic AI in software development and interest in how AI is changing the way software is built.</li> <li value="4"><span class="textBold">Ability to work effectively</span> in fast-paced environments with evolving requirements and continuous delivery practices.</li> <li value="5"><span class="textBold">Clear communication skills</span> and the ability to work effectively with engineering, product, UX, and other stakeholders across a distributed organization.</li> <li value="6"><span class="textBold">Focus on building</span> an inclusive team environment where engineers can do effective work together.</li> <li value="7"><span class="textBold">Experience with AI product development, observability, reliability engineering, Ruby, or TypeScript</span> is useful, and we welcome candidates with adjacent or transferable experience.</li> </ul> <h2>About the team</h2> <p>The <span class="textBold">Workflow</span> Catalog. We are distributed across three continents but unified by the desire to shape the future of software engineering. We're hard at work creating a product and the infrastructure that changes software development processes at scale. We're partnering with teams both within the AI space as well as all over GitLab to ensure that the Duo Agent Platform can become the backbone of many AI features at GitLab. Examples of such initiatives include Agent Skills, a reusable format for defining agent instructions that can be shared across agents, flows, CI/CD jobs, and IDE chat, and automatic flow generation that lets users describe what they want in plain language rather than having to learn the underlying YAML syntax.</p> <p>This is an opportunity to guide a team in one of the most exciting areas of GitLab and contribute to the fundamental changes that are happening to the SDLC right now.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h4><strong>How GitLab Supports Full-Time Employees</strong></h4> <ul> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/">Benefits to support your health, finances, and well-being</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/paid-time-off/">Flexible Paid Time Off</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/erg-guide/#:~:text=Definition%20of%20the%20TMRG%20%2D%20Team%20Member%20Resource%20Groups,-TMRGs%20are%20voluntary&amp;text=The%20purpose%20for%20this%20type,developing%20the%20sense%20of%20belonging.">Team Member Resource Groups</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/stock-options/">Equity Compensation &amp; Employee Stock Purchase Plan</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/learning-and-development/growth-and-development/">Growth and Development Fund</a></li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/#parental-leave">Parental leave</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/finance/expenses/#equipment">Home office</a> support</li> </ul> <p>Please note that we welcome interest from candidates with varying levels of experience; many successful candidates do not meet every single requirement. Additionally, studies have shown that people from <a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/#examples-of-select-underrepresented-groups">underrepresented groups</a> are less likely to apply to a job unless they meet every single qualification. If you're excited about this role, please apply and allow our recruiters to assess your application.</p> <hr> <p><strong>Country Hiring Guidelines: </strong>GitLab hires new team members in countries around the world. All of our roles are remote, however some roles may carry specific location-based eligibility requirements. Our Talent Acquisition team can help answer any questions about location after starting the recruiting process.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Privacy Policy: </strong>Please review our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/candidate-faq/recruitment-privacy-policy/" target="_blank">Recruitment Privacy Policy.</a> Your privacy is important to us.</p> <p>GitLab is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. GitLab’s policies and practices relating to recruitment, employment, career development and advancement, promotion, and retirement are based solely on merit, regardless of race, color, religion, ancestry, sex (including pregnancy, lactation, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression), national origin, age, citizenship, marital status, mental or physical disability, genetic information (including family medical history), discharge status from the military, protected veteran status (which includes disabled veterans, recently separated veterans, active duty wartime or campaign badge veterans, and Armed Forces service medal veterans), or any other basis protected by law. GitLab will not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on any of these characteristics. See also&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/people-policies/inc-usa/#equal-employment-opportunity-policy" target="_blank">GitLab’s EEO Policy</a>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/labor-and-employment-notices/#eeoc-us-equal-employment-opportunity-commission-notices" target="_blank">EEO is the Law</a>. If you have a disability or special need that requires&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/people-policies/inc-usa/#reasonable-accommodation" target="_blank">accommodation</a>, please let us know during the&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/interviewing/#adjustments-to-our-interview-process" target="_blank">recruiting process</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Engineering Manager, AST: Composition Analysis
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>GitLab is the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps. GitLab enables organizations to increase developer productivity, improve operational efficiency, reduce security and compliance risk, and accelerate digital transformation. More than 50 million registered users and more than 50% of the Fortune 100* trust GitLab to ship better, more secure software faster.</p> <p>The same principles built into our products are reflected in how our team works: we embrace AI as a core productivity multiplier, with all team members expected to incorporate AI into their daily workflows to drive efficiency, innovation, and impact. GitLab is where careers accelerate, innovation flourishes, and every voice is valued. Our high-performance culture is driven by our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/values/">values</a> and continuous knowledge exchange, enabling our team members to reach their full potential while collaborating with industry leaders to solve complex problems. <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OuZIb5zszQI">Co-create the future with us</a> as we build technology that transforms how the world develops software.</p> <p>*<em>Fortune 500® is a registered trademark of Fortune Media IP Limited, used under license. Claim based on GitLab data. Fortune 100 refers to the top 20% ranked companies in the 2025 Fortune 500 list, published in June 2025. Fortune and Fortune Media IP Limited are not affiliated with, and do not endorse products or services of GitLab.</em></p></div><h3><strong>An overview of this role</strong></h3> <p>As an Engineering Manager for Composition Analysis, you'll lead a team building the software composition analysis capabilities that help GitLab customers find and fix vulnerabilities in their application dependencies and software supply chain. You'll guide engineers working on software composition analysis and container scanning, and you'll be responsible for setting priorities, shaping product architecture, and running agile processes so that our security offerings stay effective, reliable, and easy to use in real DevSecOps environments.</p> <p>You'll balance complex, security-focused roadmaps and author project plans so that customers get a robust composition analysis experience within GitLab. In your first year, you'll drive key initiatives like auto-remediation of vulnerable packages and auto-fix breaking changes with AI, scanning unmanaged C/C++ dependencies, static reachability analysis, malicious package detection, and snippet detection for open source dependencies.</p> <p>Some examples of our projects:</p> <ul> <li>Building hyper-scale vulnerability detection engines for millions of GitLab users around the world</li> <li>Designing auto-remediation workflows for vulnerable open source and third-party dependencies</li> <li>Auto AI fixes for breaking changes that happen following dependency bumps</li> </ul> <h3><strong>What you’ll do</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Lead engineers across the Composition Analysis team, setting clear priorities and expectations.</li> <li>Drive key security initiatives, including auto-remediation of vulnerable software packages, scanning unmanaged C/C++ dependencies, static reachability analysis, and snippet detection for open source dependencies.</li> <li>Balance priorities and resources across the Composition Analysis team to ensure sustainable delivery and high-quality outcomes.</li> <li>Author and maintain project plans for epics within the Composition Analysis team, aligning work, identifying dependencies, and ensuring quality delivery.</li> <li>Run agile project management processes for the Composition Analysis team, including planning, estimation, and continuous improvement of delivery practices.</li> <li>Provide guidance on the architecture of software composition analysis solutions, ensuring they are robust, scalable, and effective.</li> <li>Collaborate closely with the Composition Analysis team to ensure consistent, high-quality approaches to application security across GitLab's platform.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>What you’ll bring</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Background leading multiple technical teams or groups, ideally in application security or cloud security</li> <li>Practical understanding of software composition analysis, including how to assess and manage risks in application dependencies</li> <li>Familiarity with containerization technologies, package managers, and dependency management systems</li> <li>Experience working with or around open source security tooling (for example, Syft, Grype, Trivy, or similar tools)</li> <li>Ability to plan and run agile project management processes for the Composition Analysis team, including coordinating priorities and dependencies.</li> <li>Skill in guiding product and architecture decisions for security scanning tools, balancing technical constraints with customer needs</li> <li>Openness to candidates with transferable experience in security engineering, DevSecOps, or vulnerability management who are motivated to grow in application security leadership</li> </ul> <h3><strong>About the team</strong></h3> <p>The Composition Analysis team at GitLab sits within our security product area and focuses on building and improving our software composition analysis capabilities across the DevSecOps platform. We own core features such as software composition analysis, container scanning, and related remediation workflows. You'll lead our distributed group of security-focused engineers as we collaborate asynchronously across time zones using GitLab itself for planning, code review, and delivery. Right now, we're focused on advancing capabilities like auto-remediation of vulnerable packages, scanning unmanaged C/C++ dependencies, static reachability analysis at the function level, and snippet detection for open source dependencies.</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>The base salary range for this role’s listed level is currently for residents of the&nbsp;United States only. This range is intended to reflect the role's base salary rate in locations throughout the US.&nbsp;Grade level and salary ranges are determined through interviews and a review of education, experience, knowledge, skills, abilities of the applicant, equity with other team members, alignment with market data,&nbsp;and geographic location.&nbsp;The base salary range does not include any bonuses, equity, or benefits. See more information on our <a class="waffle-rich-text-link" href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-link="https://bit.ly/GLBeneUS" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#1155cc;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:underline;font-family:''Arial'';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;">benefits</a><span data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#000000;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;font-family:'Arial';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;"> and </span><a class="waffle-rich-text-link" href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/stock-options/" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-link="https://bit.ly/GLequity" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#1155cc;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:underline;font-family:''Arial'';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;">equity</a>. Sales roles are also eligible for incentive pay targeted at up to 100% of the offered base salary.</em></p></div><div class="title">United States Salary Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$131,600</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$282,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><h4><strong>How GitLab Supports Full-Time Employees</strong></h4> <ul> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/">Benefits to support your health, finances, and well-being</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/paid-time-off/">Flexible Paid Time Off</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/erg-guide/#:~:text=Definition%20of%20the%20TMRG%20%2D%20Team%20Member%20Resource%20Groups,-TMRGs%20are%20voluntary&amp;text=The%20purpose%20for%20this%20type,developing%20the%20sense%20of%20belonging.">Team Member Resource Groups</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/stock-options/">Equity Compensation &amp; Employee Stock Purchase Plan</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/learning-and-development/growth-and-development/">Growth and Development Fund</a></li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/#parental-leave">Parental leave</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/finance/expenses/#equipment">Home office</a> support</li> </ul> <p>Please note that we welcome interest from candidates with varying levels of experience; many successful candidates do not meet every single requirement. Additionally, studies have shown that people from <a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/#examples-of-select-underrepresented-groups">underrepresented groups</a> are less likely to apply to a job unless they meet every single qualification. If you're excited about this role, please apply and allow our recruiters to assess your application.</p> <hr> <p><strong>Country Hiring Guidelines: </strong>GitLab hires new team members in countries around the world. All of our roles are remote, however some roles may carry specific location-based eligibility requirements. Our Talent Acquisition team can help answer any questions about location after starting the recruiting process.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Privacy Policy: </strong>Please review our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/candidate-faq/recruitment-privacy-policy/" target="_blank">Recruitment Privacy Policy.</a> Your privacy is important to us.</p> <p>GitLab is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. GitLab’s policies and practices relating to recruitment, employment, career development and advancement, promotion, and retirement are based solely on merit, regardless of race, color, religion, ancestry, sex (including pregnancy, lactation, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression), national origin, age, citizenship, marital status, mental or physical disability, genetic information (including family medical history), discharge status from the military, protected veteran status (which includes disabled veterans, recently separated veterans, active duty wartime or campaign badge veterans, and Armed Forces service medal veterans), or any other basis protected by law. GitLab will not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on any of these characteristics. See also&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/people-policies/inc-usa/#equal-employment-opportunity-policy" target="_blank">GitLab’s EEO Policy</a>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/labor-and-employment-notices/#eeoc-us-equal-employment-opportunity-commission-notices" target="_blank">EEO is the Law</a>. If you have a disability or special need that requires&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/people-policies/inc-usa/#reasonable-accommodation" target="_blank">accommodation</a>, please let us know during the&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/interviewing/#adjustments-to-our-interview-process" target="_blank">recruiting process</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Engineering Manager, Continuous Delivery
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>GitLab is the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps. GitLab enables organizations to increase developer productivity, improve operational efficiency, reduce security and compliance risk, and accelerate digital transformation. More than 50 million registered users and more than 50% of the Fortune 100* trust GitLab to ship better, more secure software faster.</p> <p>The same principles built into our products are reflected in how our team works: we embrace AI as a core productivity multiplier, with all team members expected to incorporate AI into their daily workflows to drive efficiency, innovation, and impact. GitLab is where careers accelerate, innovation flourishes, and every voice is valued. Our high-performance culture is driven by our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/values/">values</a> and continuous knowledge exchange, enabling our team members to reach their full potential while collaborating with industry leaders to solve complex problems. <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OuZIb5zszQI">Co-create the future with us</a> as we build technology that transforms how the world develops software.</p> <p>*<em>Fortune 500® is a registered trademark of Fortune Media IP Limited, used under license. Claim based on GitLab data. Fortune 100 refers to the top 20% ranked companies in the 2025 Fortune 500 list, published in June 2025. Fortune and Fortune Media IP Limited are not affiliated with, and do not endorse products or services of GitLab.</em></p></div><h2><strong>An overview of this role</strong></h2> <p>As Engineering Manager for GitLab CD, you'll build and lead a brand-new, globally distributed team at the forefront of GitLab's next generation of Continuous Deployment capabilities. This is a greenfield opportunity: you'll hire foundational engineers, shape the team's culture and ways of working, and own delivery of a first-class CD product within GitLab's DevSecOps platform.</p> <p>Your team will build a CD engine that goes beyond script execution—bringing true reconciliation, live state awareness, durable orchestration, and AI-native governance to GitLab's platform. The goal is to deliver features that enable customers to deploy software reliably, safely, and with confidence. This work sits at the intersection of GitLab's core platform and its AI strategy, making it a high-visibility, fast-moving area of the product.</p> <p>You'll partner closely with Product Management, cross-functional engineering teams, and infrastructure stakeholders to align technical decisions with customer needs and business goals, while building a team culture grounded in GitLab's values.</p> <h2><strong>What you'll do</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Build and lead a new globally distributed engineering team, creating an environment where team members can do their best work and grow their careers.</li> <li>Hire, onboard, and develop engineers who embody GitLab's values and hold themselves to a high standard of quality and ownership.</li> <li>Partner with Product Management and cross-functional engineering teams to shape the roadmap, make sound architectural decisions, and deliver against product commitments.</li> <li>Foster a culture of engineering excellence across reliability, performance, security, and maintainability, while keeping the pace expected in a fast-moving product area.</li> <li>Champion AI as a core part of how the team works, encouraging engineers to incorporate AI tools into their daily workflows to drive efficiency and innovation.</li> <li>Hold regular 1:1s, provide continuous feedback, and support engineers' professional growth through coaching and skill development.</li> <li>Drive a healthy delivery cadence by maintaining visibility into milestone progress, identifying blockers early, and proactively addressing patterns before they require escalation.</li> <li>Participate in the Incident Management on-call rotation to help ensure availability targets for GitLab.com are met.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What you'll bring</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Demonstrated leadership experience managing high-performing engineering teams, with hands-on technical credibility to provide architectural guidance and participate meaningfully in technical discussions.</li> <li>Strong background in distributed systems and durable workflow execution, including state persistence and replay patterns.</li> <li>Experience building or leading teams working on release orchestration, deployment automation, or continuous delivery at scale.</li> <li>Familiarity with Kubernetes deployment patterns and GitOps workflows, including progressive delivery strategies such as blue/green and canary deployments.</li> <li>Experience with policy-based governance and event-driven architectures in building reliable, enterprise-grade delivery systems.</li> <li>Proven ability to build and lead teams in early-stage contexts, including defining team processes, hiring foundational members, and establishing a strong team culture.</li> <li>Strong cross-functional alignment and consensus-building skills, with the ability to drive decisions and execution even in the face of ambiguity or competing priorities.</li> <li>Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with a bias toward async, handbook-first practices and transparency.</li> <li>Experience in remote-first, globally distributed organizations and comfort operating in environments with high autonomy and high accountability.</li> <li>Track record of fostering inclusive team cultures where engineers feel psychologically safe to contribute, take calculated risks, and hold each other to high standards.</li> <li>Strong alignment with GitLab's values (Collaboration, Results, Efficiency, Diversity Inclusion &amp; Belonging, Iteration, Transparency) with demonstrated examples from previous roles.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>About the team</strong></h2> <p>The GitLab CD team is a newly formed group within the Deploy stage. The team will be primarily Go engineers, with Ruby on Rails and frontend engineers joining as the product matures. As CD Engineering Manager you will work closely with adjacent teams across Verify, Runner, and Infrastructure, building in a collaborative, async-first environment where engineers take real ownership of outcomes. This is an exciting opportunity to help shape something new within an established company operating at significant scale.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><h4><strong>How GitLab Supports Full-Time Employees</strong></h4> <ul> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/">Benefits to support your health, finances, and well-being</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/paid-time-off/">Flexible Paid Time Off</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/erg-guide/#:~:text=Definition%20of%20the%20TMRG%20%2D%20Team%20Member%20Resource%20Groups,-TMRGs%20are%20voluntary&amp;text=The%20purpose%20for%20this%20type,developing%20the%20sense%20of%20belonging.">Team Member Resource Groups</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/stock-options/">Equity Compensation &amp; Employee Stock Purchase Plan</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/learning-and-development/growth-and-development/">Growth and Development Fund</a></li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/#parental-leave">Parental leave</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/finance/expenses/#equipment">Home office</a> support</li> </ul> <p>Please note that we welcome interest from candidates with varying levels of experience; many successful candidates do not meet every single requirement. Additionally, studies have shown that people from <a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/#examples-of-select-underrepresented-groups">underrepresented groups</a> are less likely to apply to a job unless they meet every single qualification. If you're excited about this role, please apply and allow our recruiters to assess your application.</p> <hr> <p><strong>Country Hiring Guidelines: </strong>GitLab hires new team members in countries around the world. All of our roles are remote, however some roles may carry specific location-based eligibility requirements. Our Talent Acquisition team can help answer any questions about location after starting the recruiting process.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Privacy Policy: </strong>Please review our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/candidate-faq/recruitment-privacy-policy/" target="_blank">Recruitment Privacy Policy.</a> Your privacy is important to us.</p> <p>GitLab is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. GitLab’s policies and practices relating to recruitment, employment, career development and advancement, promotion, and retirement are based solely on merit, regardless of race, color, religion, ancestry, sex (including pregnancy, lactation, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression), national origin, age, citizenship, marital status, mental or physical disability, genetic information (including family medical history), discharge status from the military, protected veteran status (which includes disabled veterans, recently separated veterans, active duty wartime or campaign badge veterans, and Armed Forces service medal veterans), or any other basis protected by law. GitLab will not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on any of these characteristics. See also&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/people-policies/inc-usa/#equal-employment-opportunity-policy" target="_blank">GitLab’s EEO Policy</a>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/labor-and-employment-notices/#eeoc-us-equal-employment-opportunity-commission-notices" target="_blank">EEO is the Law</a>. If you have a disability or special need that requires&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/people-policies/inc-usa/#reasonable-accommodation" target="_blank">accommodation</a>, please let us know during the&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/interviewing/#adjustments-to-our-interview-process" target="_blank">recruiting process</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Engineering Manager, Infrastructure Platforms
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>GitLab is the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps. GitLab enables organizations to increase developer productivity, improve operational efficiency, reduce security and compliance risk, and accelerate digital transformation. More than 50 million registered users and more than 50% of the Fortune 100* trust GitLab to ship better, more secure software faster.</p> <p>The same principles built into our products are reflected in how our team works: we embrace AI as a core productivity multiplier, with all team members expected to incorporate AI into their daily workflows to drive efficiency, innovation, and impact. GitLab is where careers accelerate, innovation flourishes, and every voice is valued. Our high-performance culture is driven by our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/values/">values</a> and continuous knowledge exchange, enabling our team members to reach their full potential while collaborating with industry leaders to solve complex problems. <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OuZIb5zszQI">Co-create the future with us</a> as we build technology that transforms how the world develops software.</p> <p>*<em>Fortune 500® is a registered trademark of Fortune Media IP Limited, used under license. Claim based on GitLab data. Fortune 100 refers to the top 20% ranked companies in the 2025 Fortune 500 list, published in June 2025. Fortune and Fortune Media IP Limited are not affiliated with, and do not endorse products or services of GitLab.</em></p></div><h1><span style="font-size: 14pt;"><strong>An overview of this role</strong></span></h1> <p>As an Engineering Manager at GitLab, you see your team as your most valuable product. Your primary focus is on the people you lead—guiding them, nurturing their growth, and ensuring they have everything they need to succeed. While you're technically credible and understand the details of the work being done, your role is about empowering a world-class engineering team, fostering their health, and driving delivery on product commitments.</p> <p>In this role, you will lead the <strong>Tenant Services, Geo</strong> team. <a href="https://docs.gitlab.com/administration/geo/">Geo</a> is a feature that replicates data from a GitLab instance to a warm-standby, used as a solution in data migrations and for disaster recovery. The Tenant Services Geo team will be responsible for supporting <a href="https://about.gitlab.com/dedicated/">GitLab Dedicated</a> customer migrations and Geo-related escalations across GitLab.com’s Dedicated offering, excluding FedRAMP environments. The team’s mandate spans the full Geo operational surface—pre- and post-cutover data hygiene, migration execution, and non-migration Geo escalations—working closely with the core Geo team, Dedicated migrations, and Support. The team will also contribute small fixes and improvements to Geo.</p> <p>Challenges in this role include growing top-tier SRE talent in India, coordinating a shift and weekend coverage model for high-risk cutovers,, and ensuring high-quality execution of migrations that carry significant customer data and reputational risk. You’ll work closely with engineering leadership, the core Geo team, Dedicated migrations, Support, and backend/SRE engineers across Infrastructure.</p> <p>Some examples of our focus areas:</p> <ul> <li>Standing up the <strong>Tenant Services, Geo</strong> shift model and rotation for Dedicated cutovers across EMEA and US hours.</li> <li>Evolving a <strong>retryable, low-risk cutover model</strong> for Dedicated migrations, in partnership with core Geo and Dedicated leadership.</li> <li>Reducing <strong>cutover duration</strong> to unlock more weekday migrations and improve team sustainability.</li> <li>Driving operational metrics like escalation absorption, internal escalation rate, cutover coverage, and response times.</li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-size: 14pt;"><strong>What you’ll do</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li><strong>Hire and manage</strong> a<a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/learning-and-development/manager-development/high-performing-teams/"> high-performing team</a> of Site Reliability Engineers in India that lives our values.</li> <li>Hold regular<a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/leadership/1-1/"> 1:1s</a> with all members of your team, providing coaching and regular feedback around the<a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/leadership/1-1/suggested-agenda-format/"> individual’s performance</a>.</li> <li>Coordinate and continuously refine the team’s <strong>shift and weekend coverage model</strong> for Dedicated migrations.</li> <li>Own operational execution of <strong>Dedicated Geo migrations and cutovers</strong>, including planning, pre-cutover preparation, live execution, and post-cutover validation and cleanup.</li> <li>Ensure the team provides high-quality, timely responses to <strong>Geo-related escalations</strong> from Support and internal partners.</li> <li>Foster technical decision making on the team, stepping in to make final decisions when necessary—especially during high-stakes migrations or incidents.</li> <li>Build and maintain <strong>runbooks, guardrails, and post-cutover reviews</strong> so the team operates with rigor rather than improvisation, especially during ramp-up.</li> <li>Collaborate with core Geo, Dedicated migrations, and other Infrastructure teams to identify and prioritize <strong>engineering investments</strong> that improve migration tooling and processes.</li> <li>Define, track, and report on <strong>key operational metrics</strong> such as escalation volume absorbed, internal escalation rate, cutover coverage, response times, and team health signals, using them to drive continuous improvement.</li> <li>Participate in the<a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/engineering/infrastructure/incident-management/#incident-manager-responsibilities"> Incident Management on-call rotation</a> to help ensure availability goals for GitLab.com are met, working with reliability engineers and development team members.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What you’ll bring</strong></h2> <ul> <li>3+ years of experience <strong>managing SRE, infrastructure, or platform engineering teams</strong> operating highly-available distributed systems at scale, ideally in a SaaS environment with customer-facing SLAs.</li> <li>Demonstrated ability to lead in a <strong>remote, high-performance environment</strong>, collaborating across multiple time zones and cultures.</li> <li>Experience running or significantly contributing to <strong>large-scale data migrations</strong> where customer data integrity and downtime risk must be carefully managed.</li> <li>Strong infrastructure background, including <strong>cloud platforms, observability, incident response, and distributed multi-tenant architectures</strong>.</li> <li>Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to translate complex technical concepts and risk trade-offs into clear, actionable insight for both technical and non-technical stakeholders, including customers.</li> <li>Strong problem-solving abilities and attention to detail, with a focus on delivering <strong>high-quality, low-risk operational outcomes</strong> in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.</li> <li>Alignment with our company values and a commitment to working in accordance with those values.</li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-size: 14pt;"><strong>It’s a Plus if You Have</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li>Experience working in or with <strong>managed/hosted environments</strong> similar to GitLab Dedicated, including regulated or compliance-sensitive customers (e.g., SOC2, ISO).</li> <li>Working knowledge of technologies commonly used in SRE and migration workflows (e.g., Kubernetes, Terraform, observability stacks, scripting languages).</li> <li>Used GitLab for personal or professional projects, and/or contributed to open source projects.</li> <li>Past experience working in an enterprise developer tools company or a high-growth infrastructure product company.</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><h4><strong>How GitLab Supports Full-Time Employees</strong></h4> <ul> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/">Benefits to support your health, finances, and well-being</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/paid-time-off/">Flexible Paid Time Off</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/erg-guide/#:~:text=Definition%20of%20the%20TMRG%20%2D%20Team%20Member%20Resource%20Groups,-TMRGs%20are%20voluntary&amp;text=The%20purpose%20for%20this%20type,developing%20the%20sense%20of%20belonging.">Team Member Resource Groups</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/stock-options/">Equity Compensation &amp; Employee Stock Purchase Plan</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/learning-and-development/growth-and-development/">Growth and Development Fund</a></li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/#parental-leave">Parental leave</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/finance/expenses/#equipment">Home office</a> support</li> </ul> <p>Please note that we welcome interest from candidates with varying levels of experience; many successful candidates do not meet every single requirement. Additionally, studies have shown that people from <a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/#examples-of-select-underrepresented-groups">underrepresented groups</a> are less likely to apply to a job unless they meet every single qualification. If you're excited about this role, please apply and allow our recruiters to assess your application.</p> <hr> <p><strong>Country Hiring Guidelines: </strong>GitLab hires new team members in countries around the world. All of our roles are remote, however some roles may carry specific location-based eligibility requirements. Our Talent Acquisition team can help answer any questions about location after starting the recruiting process.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Privacy Policy: </strong>Please review our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/candidate-faq/recruitment-privacy-policy/" target="_blank">Recruitment Privacy Policy.</a> Your privacy is important to us.</p> <p>GitLab is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. GitLab’s policies and practices relating to recruitment, employment, career development and advancement, promotion, and retirement are based solely on merit, regardless of race, color, religion, ancestry, sex (including pregnancy, lactation, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression), national origin, age, citizenship, marital status, mental or physical disability, genetic information (including family medical history), discharge status from the military, protected veteran status (which includes disabled veterans, recently separated veterans, active duty wartime or campaign badge veterans, and Armed Forces service medal veterans), or any other basis protected by law. GitLab will not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on any of these characteristics. See also&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/people-policies/inc-usa/#equal-employment-opportunity-policy" target="_blank">GitLab’s EEO Policy</a>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/labor-and-employment-notices/#eeoc-us-equal-employment-opportunity-commission-notices" target="_blank">EEO is the Law</a>. jobs.location: Remote, Canada; Remote, Israel; Remote, Netherlands; Remote, United Kingdom; Remote, US
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>GitLab is the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps. GitLab enables organizations to increase developer productivity, improve operational efficiency, reduce security and compliance risk, and accelerate digital transformation. More than 50 million registered users and more than 50% of the Fortune 100* trust GitLab to ship better, more secure software faster.</p> <p>The same principles built into our products are reflected in how our team works: we embrace AI as a core productivity multiplier, with all team members expected to incorporate AI into their daily workflows to drive efficiency, innovation, and impact. GitLab is where careers accelerate, innovation flourishes, and every voice is valued. Our high-performance culture is driven by our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/values/">values</a> and continuous knowledge exchange, enabling our team members to reach their full potential while collaborating with industry leaders to solve complex problems. <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OuZIb5zszQI">Co-create the future with us</a> as we build technology that transforms how the world develops software.</p> <p>*<em>Fortune 500® is a registered trademark of Fortune Media IP Limited, used under license. Claim based on GitLab data. Fortune 100 refers to the top 20% ranked companies in the 2025 Fortune 500 list, published in June 2025. Fortune and Fortune Media IP Limited are not affiliated with, and do not endorse products or services of GitLab.</em></p></div><h4>An overview of this role</h4> <p>As the Engineering Manager, Software Supply Chain Security: Pipeline Security, you’ll lead a team that makes GitLab CI pipelines more secure and trustworthy for thousands of organizations. You’ll guide the design and delivery of Software Supply Chain Security features, with a primary focus on CI job artifact security. This includes implementing the SLSA (Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts) framework in GitLab CI/CD and integrating related capabilities like SBOM, software composition analysis, and vulnerability management. You’ll treat your team as your product, safeguarding team health, hiring and developing a high-performing group of engineers, and collaborating closely with Product Management and Security to deliver on roadmap commitments. Together, you’ll improve how users protect their software supply chains in their first year and beyond.</p> <p>Some examples of our projects:</p> <ul> <li value="1"><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/direction/software_supply_chain_security/pipeline_security/secrets_management/" target="_blank">Developing a native secrets management system for GitLab CI pipelines</a></li> <li value="2"><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/direction/software_supply_chain_security/pipeline_security/artifact_security/" target="_blank">Implementing SLSA Level 3 compliance features for CI job artifacts</a></li> </ul> <h4>What you’ll do</h4> <ul> <li value="1">Lead a team of engineers building Software Supply Chain Security features with a focus on CI job artifact security.</li> <li value="2">Guide the design and implementation of SLSA (Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts) compliance within GitLab CI/CD pipelines.</li> <li value="3">Collaborate with Product Managers to define, prioritize, and deliver the roadmap for supply chain security capabilities.</li> <li value="4">Partner with Security team members to ensure new and existing features meet GitLab’s security standards and align with best practices.</li> <li value="5">Stay current with software supply chain security standards and tools, including SLSA, SBOM, software composition analysis, and vulnerability management. Translate what you learn into actionable product improvements.</li> <li value="6">Educate and advocate for supply chain security best practices across engineering teams to drive adoption of secure patterns in CI pipelines.</li> <li value="7">Represent the Pipeline Security team in cross-functional initiatives and, when appropriate, in external industry forums focused on software supply chain security.</li> <li value="8">Drive continuous improvement in team health, delivery predictability, and documentation quality for pipeline and supply chain security features.</li> </ul> <h4>What you’ll bring</h4> <ul> <li value="1">Experience leading and developing engineering teams, with a focus on building secure, reliable product features.</li> <li value="2">Practical knowledge of software supply chain security concepts, tools, and industry standards.</li> <li value="3">Understanding of the SLSA (Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts) framework and how to apply it in CI/CD pipelines.</li> <li value="4">Familiarity with software artifact provenance, attestation, and verification techniques.</li> <li value="5">Knowledge of secure software development practices, including container security, software composition analysis, and vulnerability management.</li> <li value="6">Experience working with CI/CD systems and their security considerations.</li> <li value="7">Ability to collaborate effectively with product management, security, and other cross-functional partners, and to advocate for supply chain security best practices.</li> <li value="8">Openness to learning new technologies and approaches, with transferable skills from related security, infrastructure, or software engineering domains.</li> </ul> <h4>About the team</h4> <p>Our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/engineering/development/sec/software-supply-chain-security/pipeline-security/" target="_blank">Pipeline Security</a> team is a globally distributed group of engineers who collaborate asynchronously across time zones. We're focused on building Software Supply Chain Security features into the core GitLab platform, with current priorities including native secrets management for CI pipelines, artifact provenance and verification, and achieving SLSA Level 3 compliance. We partner closely with Product, Security, and other stage groups to design and implement these capabilities. We value clear communication, thorough documentation, and making new features straightforward for users to adopt.</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>The base salary range for this role’s listed level is currently for residents of the&nbsp;United States only. This range is intended to reflect the role's base salary rate in locations throughout the US.&nbsp;Grade level and salary ranges are determined through interviews and a review of education, experience, knowledge, skills, abilities of the applicant, equity with other team members, alignment with market data,&nbsp;and geographic location.&nbsp;The base salary range does not include any bonuses, equity, or benefits. See more information on our <a class="waffle-rich-text-link" href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-link="https://bit.ly/GLBeneUS" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#1155cc;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:underline;font-family:''Arial'';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;">benefits</a><span data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#000000;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;font-family:'Arial';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;"> and </span><a class="waffle-rich-text-link" href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/stock-options/" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-link="https://bit.ly/GLequity" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#1155cc;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:underline;font-family:''Arial'';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;">equity</a>. Sales roles are also eligible for incentive pay targeted at up to 100% of the offered base salary.</em></p></div><div class="title">United States Salary Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$131,600</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$282,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><h4><strong>How GitLab Supports Full-Time Employees</strong></h4> <ul> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/">Benefits to support your health, finances, and well-being</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/paid-time-off/">Flexible Paid Time Off</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/erg-guide/#:~:text=Definition%20of%20the%20TMRG%20%2D%20Team%20Member%20Resource%20Groups,-TMRGs%20are%20voluntary&amp;text=The%20purpose%20for%20this%20type,developing%20the%20sense%20of%20belonging.">Team Member Resource Groups</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/stock-options/">Equity Compensation &amp; Employee Stock Purchase Plan</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/learning-and-development/growth-and-development/">Growth and Development Fund</a></li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/#parental-leave">Parental leave</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/finance/expenses/#equipment">Home office</a> support</li> </ul> <p>Please note that we welcome interest from candidates with varying levels of experience; many successful candidates do not meet every single requirement. Additionally, studies have shown that people from <a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/#examples-of-select-underrepresented-groups">underrepresented groups</a> are less likely to apply to a job unless they meet every single qualification. If you're excited about this role, please apply and allow our recruiters to assess your application.</p> <hr> <p><strong>Country Hiring Guidelines: </strong>GitLab hires new team members in countries around the world. All of our roles are remote, however some roles may carry specific location-based eligibility requirements. Our Talent Acquisition team can help answer any questions about location after starting the recruiting process.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Privacy Policy: </strong>Please review our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/candidate-faq/recruitment-privacy-policy/" target="_blank">Recruitment Privacy Policy.</a> Your privacy is important to us.</p> <p>GitLab is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. GitLab will not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on any of these characteristics.
