jobs.title: Deal Support Quality & Strategy Manager
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><strong>Who we are</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Samsara (NYSE: IOT) is the pioneer of the Connected Operations™ Cloud, which is a platform that enables organizations that depend on physical operations to harness Internet of Things (IoT) data to develop actionable insights and improve their operations. At Samsara, we are helping improve the safety, efficiency and sustainability of the physical operations that power our global economy. Representing more than 40% of global GDP, these industries are the infrastructure of our planet, including agriculture, construction, field services, transportation, and manufacturing — and we are excited to help digitally transform their operations at scale.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Working at Samsara means you’ll help define the future of physical operations and be on a team that’s shaping an exciting array of product solutions, including Video-Based Safety, Vehicle Telematics, Apps and Driver Workflows, and Equipment Monitoring. As part of a recently public company, you’ll have the autonomy and support to make an impact as we build for the long term.</span></p></div><h4><strong>About the Role</strong></h4> <p>Samsara is looking for an experienced Deal Support Quality &amp; Strategy Manager to play a pivotal role in ensuring operational excellence across our Deal Support (Deal Execution + Deal Strategy) organization. The Deal Support Quality &amp; Strategy Manager will partner with leadership to build, implement, and maintain a quality program that supports the accuracy, efficiency, and consistency of our deal lifecycle processes. You will be responsible for providing feedback and business intelligence to drive continuous improvement and mitigate risk.</p> <p>The ideal candidate has expertise in quality assurance operations, a keen eye for detail, and experience analyzing data to identify opportunities that have a direct impact on business outcomes. This role will join a team in building mode, and a successful candidate will have a strong bias for action and help create a sustainable quality culture.</p> <p class="p1">This role is open to candidates residing in the US <span class="s2"><strong>except</strong></span> the San Francisco Bay Metro Area, NYC Metro Area, and Washington, D.C. Metro Area.</p> <h4><strong>You should apply if:</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>You want to impact the industries that run our world:</strong> Your efforts will result in real-world impact—helping to keep the lights on, get food into grocery stores, reduce emissions, and most importantly, ensure workers return home safely.</li> <li><strong>You are the architect of your own career:</strong> If you put in the work, this role won’t be your last at Samsara. We set up our employees for success and have built a culture that encourages rapid career development and countless opportunities to experiment and master your craft.</li> <li><strong>You have an innate curiosity about how businesses work:</strong> You will learn the ins and outs of complex sales processes and go-to-market operations, and you'll be energized by finding ways to make them more efficient.</li> <li><strong>You want to be with the best:</strong> At Samsara, we win together, celebrate together and support each other. You will be surrounded by a high-caliber team that will encourage you to do your best.</li> <li><strong>You are a team player: </strong>At Samsara, we help each other out by sharing best practices and focusing on winning as a team.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>In this role, you will:</strong></h4> <ul> <li><strong>Process Auditing</strong>: Observe and audit deal-related interactions and workflows across various internal systems to assess performance, adherence to policy, and identify trends</li> <li><strong>Quality Assurance &amp; Coaching</strong>: Utilize best practices and a standardized rubric to ensure the highest standards of accuracy and service are being delivered. Design and support a coaching framework that enables managers to deliver impactful feedback to their teams</li> <li><strong>Insight &amp; Analysis</strong>: Leverage Excel, Google Sheets, and similar tools to analyze quality data, track metrics, and derive actionable insights. Deliver data-driven recommendations on service and process quality to Deal Support leadership</li> <li><strong>Program Support</strong>: Support the Deal Support Quality Program through targeted auditing, designing agile monitoring forms to collect qualitative insights, and leading investigative projects that identify opportunities to improve behaviors, processes, and tools</li> <li><strong>Collaboration</strong>: Work collaboratively with cross-functional partners in Sales, Sales Operations, and Systems teams to improve processes and achieve consistent outcomes for quality initiatives</li> <li><strong>Champion, role model, and embed Samsara’s cultural principles</strong> (Focus on Customer Success, Build for the Long Term, Adopt a Growth Mindset, Be Inclusive, Win as a Team) as we scale globally and across new offices</li> </ul> <h4><strong>Minimum requirements for the role:</strong></h4> <ul> <li>3-5 years of experience in a quality assurance, process improvement, or operations role, with at least 2 years directly focused on executing quality programs</li> <li>Demonstrated experience in designing and using quality assurance rubrics or scorecards to perform audits and provide structured feedback</li> <li>Strong analytical skills with proficiency in root cause analysis, identifying error trends, and using data to tell a story about process performance</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with an ability to deliver constructive feedback and present findings clearly to stakeholders</li> <li>A meticulous attention to detail and a passion for continuous process improvement</li> <li>Ability to work independently in a dynamic and fast-paced environment</li> <li>Experience leveraging AI tools to automate quality workflows or analyze large datasets</li> </ul> <h4><strong>An ideal candidate also has</strong></h4> <ul> <li>Industry-standard quality or process improvement certifications (e.g., COPC, Six Sigma, ASQ)</li> <li>Experience incorporating stakeholder feedback (like CSAT or internal surveys) into the quality improvement process</li> <li>Experience facilitating calibration sessions with stakeholders to ensure scoring consistency and alignment</li> <li>Experience with software used for quality assurance tracking and reporting</li> <li>Familiarity with the sales deal lifecycle or a business operations environment is a strong plus</li> <li>Experience with Salesforce is preferred, but not required</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><span style="font-size: 16px;">The range of annual base salary for full-time employees for this position is below. Please note that base pay offered may vary depending on factors including your city of residence, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Learn more about our total rewards and benefits below.</span></p></div><div class="title">Annual Base Salary</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$94,010</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$142,200 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Total Rewards</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we build for the people who keep the global economy moving. We want owners, not passengers, which is why <a href="https://www.samsara.com/company/careers/benefits">our rewards</a> are designed to fuel high-impact builders. Our compensation program delivers above-market total compensation through a combination of base salary, performance-based bonus/variable pay, and equity (for eligible roles) in a high-growth public company. We meaningfully differentiate pay for our top performers, who have the opportunity to earn above-market compensation that can outpace the broader market over time.</p> <p>Beyond compensation, we provide the foundations that enable long-term success: a flexible, employee-led remote model, a professional development stipend, comprehensive health and parental leave plans, and more. If you’re ready to build for the long term and own the outcome, your journey starts here.</p> <p><strong>Flexible Working&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Samsara, we embrace a flexible working model that caters to the diverse needs of our teams. Our offices are open for those who prefer to work in-person and we also support remote work where it aligns with our operational requirements. For certain positions, being close to one of our offices or within a specific geographic area is important to facilitate collaboration, access to resources, or alignment with our service regions. In these cases, the job description will clearly indicate any working location requirements. Our goal is to ensure that all members of our team can contribute effectively, whether they are working on-site, in a hybrid model, or fully remotely. All offers of employment are contingent upon an individual’s ability to secure and maintain the legal right to work at the company and in the specified work location, if applicable.</span></p> <p><strong>Belonging at Samsara</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we welcome everyone regardless of their background. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, and other characteristics protected by law. We depend on the unique approaches of our team members to help us solve complex problems and want to ensure that Samsara is a place where people from all backgrounds can make an impact.</p> <p><strong>Accommodations&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Samsara is an inclusive work environment, and we are committed to ensuring equal opportunity in employment for qualified persons with disabilities. Please email <a href="mailto:accessibleinterviewing@samsara.com">accessibleinterviewing@samsara.com</a> or <a href="https://forms.gle/DrM6DFQUiSgfPtCVA">click here</a> if you require any reasonable accommodations throughout the recruiting process.</p> <p><strong>Our Commitment to Authenticity</strong></p> <p>We use <a href="https://hiretofu.com/privacyPolicy">Tofu</a>, a fraud detection tool, to validate the authenticity of applications and protect against identity fraud. This ensures we are connecting with real people and allows us to prioritize genuine candidates. Please see Samsara’s <a href="https://www.samsara.com/legal/candidate-privacy-notice">Candidate Privacy Notice</a> for more information.</p> <p><strong>Fraudulent Employment Offers</strong></p> <p>Samsara is aware of scams involving fake job interviews and offers. Please know we do not charge fees to applicants at any stage of the hiring process. Official communication about your application will only come from emails ending in @samsara.com, @us-greenhouse-mail.io or @mail3.guide.co. For more information regarding fraudulent employment offers, please visit our&nbsp;<a href="https://samsara.com/blog/avoiding-fake-job-offers">blog post here</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Installation Specialist
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><strong>Who we are</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Samsara (NYSE: IOT) is the pioneer of the Connected Operations™ Cloud, which is a platform that enables organizations that depend on physical operations to harness Internet of Things (IoT) data to develop actionable insights and improve their operations. Representing more than 40% of global GDP, these industries are the infrastructure of our planet, including agriculture, construction, field services, transportation, and manufacturing — and we are excited to help digitally transform their operations at scale.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Working at Samsara means you’ll help define the future of physical operations and be on a team that’s shaping an exciting array of product solutions, including Video-Based Safety, Vehicle Telematics, Apps and Driver Workflows, and Equipment Monitoring. As part of a recently public company, you’ll have the autonomy and support to make an impact as we build for the long term.</span></p></div><p><strong>About the role:</strong></p> <p>Samsara’s Installation Specialists are on the front lines of ensuring customers successfully deploy our hardware and technology at scale. In this role, you will oversee customer engagements with installation partners, provide onsite management for complex deployments, and proactively de-risk projects to deliver smooth, high-quality installations. You’ll work closely with customers, partners, and cross-functional teams to ensure every project drives adoption and long-term value.</p> <p>This role is ideal for someone with ~4 years of experience in project management, installation oversight, or field services who thrives in fast-paced, customer-facing environments.</p> <p><strong>This is a remote position&nbsp;open to candidates residing in the US <span style="text-decoration: underline;">except</span> the San Francisco Bay Metro Area, NYC Metro Area, and Washington, D.C. Metro Area. &nbsp; &nbsp;</strong></p> <p><strong>You should apply if:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>You want to impact the industries that run our world:</strong> Every phone call you answer and every email you send can affect whether truck drivers deliver goods on time and without accidents, whether students get dropped off safely from school, or whether power gets restored quickly after a natural disaster.</li> <li><strong>You thrive the most when solving problems: </strong>Our constantly expanding technology and the complexities faced by our customers provide an exciting range of challenges for our Customer Success teams. With a growth mindset and a desire to learn, you will strategically partner with our customers to find unique solutions to help keep their operations safe, efficient, and sustainable.</li> <li><strong>You are a natural relationship builder:</strong> Whether the relationship is with our customers or with cross-functional teams in Samsara, you are in constant communication and collaboration with key stakeholders to win as a team.</li> <li><strong>You are the architect of your own career: </strong>If you put in the work, this role won’t be your last at Samsara. This Customer Success team is still shaping its future and you will have plenty of autonomy and opportunities to master your craft in a hyper growth environment.</li> <li><strong>You want to be with the best: </strong>At Samsara, we win together, celebrate together and support each other. You will be surrounded by the best and brightest professionals out there.</li> </ul> <h4><strong>In this role, you will:</strong>&nbsp;</h4> <ul> <li>Monitor customer installation milestone progress in collaboration with Samsara Implementation Consultant, spearheading prompt resolution to issues, installation challenges, partner and project complexities while ensuring quality standards are always met.</li> <li>Serve as a point of contact for escalations and troubleshooting during install phases.</li> <li>Monitor CSAT related to installations and resolve root causes of dissatisfaction.</li> <li>Assist partners in building project-specific installation guides using Samsara best practices and deliver onsite training to installer technicians on project specific requirements</li> <li>Coordinate with installation vendors to ensure work is completed on time, on budget, and to Samsara’s quality standards</li> <li>Provide onsite oversight for large or complex deployments, including site readiness checks, site-level access and communication channels with installers, real-time troubleshooting, and onsite quality checks</li> <li>Perform first of kind installations on complex customer projects, serving as the hands on technical expert</li> <li>Identify risks early, propose mitigation strategies, and keep projects on track</li> <li>Capture lessons learned from each project to improve processes and drive continuous improvement with installation partners</li> <li>Collaborate with internal teams (Customer Success, Implementation, and Product) to escalate, share feedback, and enhance the installation experience</li> <li>Ensure compliance with safety standards and Samsara best practices throughout the installation lifecycle</li> <li>Ensure that the Statement of Work (SOW) is fully aligned to the project’s installation requirements</li> <li>Champion, role model, and embed Samsara’s cultural principles (Focus on Customer Success, Build for the Long Term, Adopt a Growth Mindset, Be Inclusive, Win as a Team) as we scale globally and across new offices</li> </ul> <p><strong>Minimum requirements for this role:</strong></p> <ul> <li>High school diploma required, advanced degree preferred.</li> <li>Must have valid driver's license and be insurable&nbsp;</li> <li>3+ years experience managing vehicle and/or hardware technology.</li> <li>Prior vehicle installation or up-fitter experience preferred (e.g. experience installing emergency lighting, two-way mobile radios, GPS equipment, antennas or other electrical equipment).&nbsp;</li> <li>Understanding of vehicle diagnostic systems, including Controller Area Networks (CAN bus) and Power Take Off (PTO) applications, for on-road and off-road vehicles and yellow iron equipment.</li> <li>Basic understanding of electronics and electrical systems.</li> <li>Experience in field operations coordination, implementation, and technical deployment.</li> <li>Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage multiple projects in parallel.</li> <li>Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.</li> <li>Fluent in English (verbal and written).</li> <li>Comfortable in a dynamic, fast-paced environment with cross-functional collaboration.</li> <li>Customer travel is expected up to 50-70%, including weekends and overnight stays.</li> </ul> <p><strong>An ideal candidate also:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Can develop professional customer-facing documentation and communicate effectively in ambiguous, fast-paced work environments</li> <li>Has strong bias for standardization &amp; automation, the ability to think big, with insistence on high standards.</li> <li>Thrives in a fast-paced and change-heavy environment.</li> <li>Possesses strong problem-solving skills and a continuous improvement mindset.</li> <li>Has familiarity or hands-on experience with AI tools to drive operational efficiency.</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><span style="font-size: 16px;">The range of annual base salary for full-time employees for this position is below. Learn more about our total rewards and benefits below.</span></p></div><div class="title">Annual Base Salary</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$76,160</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$132,480 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Total Rewards</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we build for the people who keep the global economy moving. jobs.title: Renewals Account Executive - Enterprise - Mexico
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><strong>Who we are</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Samsara (NYSE: IOT) is the pioneer of the Connected Operations™ Cloud, which is a platform that enables organizations that depend on physical operations to harness Internet of Things (IoT) data to develop actionable insights and improve their operations. Representing more than 40% of global GDP, these industries are the infrastructure of our planet, including agriculture, construction, field services, transportation, and manufacturing — and we are excited to help digitally transform their operations at scale.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Working at Samsara means you’ll help define the future of physical operations and be on a team that’s shaping an exciting array of product solutions, including Video-Based Safety, Vehicle Telematics, Apps and Driver Workflows, and Equipment Monitoring. As part of a recently public company, you’ll have the autonomy and support to make an impact as we build for the long term.</span></p></div><p><strong>About this role:</strong></p> <p>Renewals are the heartbeat of Samsara’s long-term success. As a Renewals Account Executive for our Mexico Enterprise segment, you will lead the commercial strategy for a portfolio of 20-45 high-value accounts per quarter. This is a high-impact role where you will be responsible for securing favourable renewal terms while minimizing financial attrition, identifying growth opportunities, passing findings and insights back to the business, and closing renewal deals.</p> <p>Your mission is to balance efficiency with high-touch strategic negotiation, ensuring our Mexico customers continue to see Samsara as their "source of truth" and long term partner for physical operations.</p> <p><strong>You should apply if:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li><strong>You want to impact the industries that run our world:</strong> Your efforts result in real-world impact—helping to keep the lights on, get food into grocery stores, and ensure workers return home safely. You have an innate curiosity about how businesses work, whether you’re diving into the inner workings of a food distribution center or a waste management firm to understand how our technology makes them better.</li> <li><strong>You thrive in a competitive arena and play to win:</strong> You aren't intimidated by a "save" cycle; you lean into it. You are a creative thinker who can confidently advocate for a customer’s resources at time of renewal and prove why Samsara remains their best-in-class partner. You expect to win because you believe in our value, and you have the grit to defend our footprint against any competitor.</li> <li><strong>You are a Master of Prioritization</strong>: You know how to triage a book of business, while dedicating deep strategic work to your six-figure "anchor" accounts. You take pride in a clean forecast, and know that deal execution is an essential part of hitting your numbers.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>You are a Value Architect who builds genuine relationships:</strong> You don’t just "process" a contract; you re-confirm the business case. You build trust quickly, and you can articulate ROI to a CFO or operational value to a Fleet Manager with equal fluency.</li> <li><strong>You can navigate internal complexity to win as a team</strong>: You recognize that sales is a team sport. You are comfortable collaborating with CS, Deal Desk, Legal, and Finance to navigate custom terms and "get to yes" on agreements, all while sharing best practices to help the broader team succeed.</li> <li><strong>You want to be with the best:</strong> You thrive in a high-performance culture where you are surrounded by top-tier talent. You are looking for a role that challenges you to go farther than you have before, moving with urgency and a growth mindset to secure the revenue that fuels Samsara’s mission.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>In this role, you will:</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Own the Mexico Enterprise Market:</strong> Act as the primary commercial lead for a portfolio of 50–75 renewals per quarter. You will manage high-touch accounts ranging from <strong>500k+</strong>, serving as a dedicated expert on the Mexican business landscape, local competitors, and regional market trends.</li> <li><strong>Drive Full-Cycle Commercial Execution:</strong> Lead every stage of the renewal process, from initial quoting and complex negotiations to securing internal approvals. You will prioritize <strong>36-month terms</strong> to stabilize the partnership and maximize long-term Lifetime Value (LTV).</li> <li><strong>Forecast with Precision: </strong>Maintain impeccable Salesforce hygiene to ensure your pipeline is a "single source of truth." You will provide leadership with scenario forecasting, clear visibility into territory risks, and data-driven insights that make your outcomes predictable.</li> <li><strong>Strategize Across the Account Team:</strong> Partner seamlessly with CSMs and AEs to map out expansion opportunities. You will bridge the gap between retention and growth, identifying expansion opportunities while ensuring a frictionless, unified experience for the customer.</li> <li><strong>Operate as a Business Owner:</strong> Take full accountability for your territory’s health. You don't just manage a list; you proactively solve for churn, advocate for the needs of the Mexico market, and lean into the "owner" mindset to hit and exceed your retention targets.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Minimum requirements for this role:</strong></h3> <ol> <li><strong>Experience:</strong> 2+ years of full-cycle closing or strategic renewals experience, specifically managing Enterprise Mexico accounts.</li> <li><strong>Commercial acumen:&nbsp;</strong>Proven track record of managing $3M+ in quarterly quota with deal sizes above $300k.</li> <li><strong>Mexico Market Context:&nbsp;</strong>Experience navigating the Mexico business landscape.</li> <li><strong>Negotiation Prowess:&nbsp;</strong>Ability to manage multi-stakeholder "save" cycles and handle complex procurement/legal requirements.</li> <li><strong>Operational Excellence:&nbsp;</strong>Expert proficiency in Salesforce and a disciplined approach to pipeline management.</li> </ol><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Total Rewards</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we build for the people who keep the global economy moving. We want owners, not passengers, which is why <a href="https://www.samsara.com/company/careers/benefits">our rewards</a> are designed to fuel high-impact builders. If you’re ready to build for the long term and own the outcome, your journey starts here.</p> <p><strong>Flexible Working&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Samsara, we embrace a flexible working model that caters to the diverse needs of our teams. jobs.title: Renewals Account Executive - Mid-Market - Mexico
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><strong>Who we are</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Samsara (NYSE: IOT) is the pioneer of the Connected Operations™ Cloud, which is a platform that enables organizations that depend on physical operations to harness Internet of Things (IoT) data to develop actionable insights and improve their operations. At Samsara, we are helping improve the safety, efficiency and sustainability of the physical operations that power our global economy. Representing more than 40% of global GDP, these industries are the infrastructure of our planet, including agriculture, construction, field services, transportation, and manufacturing — and we are excited to help digitally transform their operations at scale.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Working at Samsara means you’ll help define the future of physical operations and be on a team that’s shaping an exciting array of product solutions, including Video-Based Safety, Vehicle Telematics, Apps and Driver Workflows, and Equipment Monitoring. As part of a recently public company, you’ll have the autonomy and support to make an impact as we build for the long term.</span></p></div><p><strong>About this role:</strong></p> <p>Renewals are the heartbeat of Samsara’s long-term success. As a Renewals Account Executive for our Mexico Mid-Market segment, you will lead the commercial strategy for a portfolio of 50–75 high-value accounts per quarter. This is a high-impact role where you will be responsible for securing favourable renewal terms while minimizing financial attrition, identifying growth opportunities, passing findings and insights back to the business, and closing renewal deals.</p> <p>Your mission is to balance efficiency with high-touch strategic negotiation, ensuring our Mexico customers continue to see Samsara as their "source of truth" and long term partner for physical operations.</p> <p><strong>You should apply if:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>You want to impact the industries that run our world:</strong> Your efforts result in real-world impact—helping to keep the lights on, get food into grocery stores, and ensure workers return home safely. You have an innate curiosity about how businesses work, whether you’re diving into the inner workings of a food distribution center or a waste management firm to understand how our technology makes them better.</li> <li><strong>You thrive in a competitive arena and play to win:</strong> You aren't intimidated by a "save" cycle; you lean into it. You are a creative thinker who can confidently advocate for a customer’s resources at time of renewal and prove why Samsara remains their best-in-class partner. You expect to win because you believe in our value, and you have the grit to defend our footprint against any competitor.</li> <li><strong>You are a Master of Prioritization</strong>: You know how to triage a book of business, while dedicating deep strategic work to your six-figure "anchor" accounts. You take pride in a clean forecast, and know that deal execution is an essential part of hitting your numbers.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>You are a Value Architect who builds genuine relationships:</strong> You don’t just "process" a contract; you re-confirm the business case. You build trust quickly, and you can articulate ROI to a CFO or operational value to a Fleet Manager with equal fluency.</li> <li><strong>You can navigate internal complexity to win as a team</strong>: You recognize that sales is a team sport. You are comfortable collaborating with CS, Deal Desk, Legal, and Finance to navigate custom terms and "get to yes" on agreements, all while sharing best practices to help the broader team succeed.</li> <li><strong>You want to be with the best:</strong> You thrive in a high-performance culture where you are surrounded by top-tier talent. You are looking for a role that challenges you to go farther than you have before, moving with urgency and a growth mindset to secure the revenue that fuels Samsara’s mission.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>In this role, you will:</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Own the Mexico Mid-Market:</strong> Act as the primary commercial lead for a portfolio of 50–75 renewals per quarter. You will manage high-touch accounts ranging from <strong>$25k to $250k+</strong>, serving as a dedicated expert on the Mexican business landscape, local competitors, and regional market trends.</li> <li><strong>Drive Full-Cycle Commercial Execution:</strong> Lead every stage of the renewal process, from initial quoting and complex negotiations to securing internal approvals. You will prioritize <strong>36-month terms</strong> to stabilize the partnership and maximize long-term Lifetime Value (LTV).</li> <li><strong>Forecast with Precision: </strong>Maintain impeccable Salesforce hygiene to ensure your pipeline is a "single source of truth." You will provide leadership with scenario forecasting, clear visibility into territory risks, and data-driven insights that make your outcomes predictable.</li> <li><strong>Strategize Across the Account Team:</strong> Partner seamlessly with CSMs and AEs to map out expansion opportunities. You will bridge the gap between retention and growth, identifying expansion opportunities while ensuring a frictionless, unified experience for the customer.</li> <li><strong>Operate as a Business Owner:</strong> Take full accountability for your territory’s health. You don't just manage a list; you proactively solve for churn, advocate for the needs of the Mexico market, and lean into the "owner" mindset to hit and exceed your retention targets.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Minimum requirements for this role:</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Experience:</strong> 2+ years of full-cycle closing or strategic renewals experience, specifically managing Mid-Market or Commercial accounts.</li> <li><strong>Commercial acumen: </strong>Proven track record of managing $1M+ in quarterly quota with deal sizes above $100k</li> <li><strong>Mexico Market Context: </strong>Experience navigating the Mexico business landscape</li> <li><strong>Negotiation Prowess: </strong>Ability to manage multi-stakeholder "save" cycles and handle complex procurement/legal requirements</li> <li><strong>Operational Excellence:&nbsp;</strong>Expert proficiency in Salesforce and a disciplined approach to pipeline management.</li> </ul><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Total Rewards</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we build for the people who keep the global economy moving. We want owners, not passengers, which is why <a href="https://www.samsara.com/company/careers/benefits">our rewards</a> are designed to fuel high-impact builders. Our compensation program delivers above-market total compensation through a combination of base salary, performance-based bonus/variable pay, and equity (for eligible roles) in a high-growth public company. We meaningfully differentiate pay for our top performers, who have the opportunity to earn above-market compensation that can outpace the broader market over time.</p> <p>Beyond compensation, we provide the foundations that enable long-term success: a flexible, employee-led remote model, a professional development stipend, comprehensive health and parental leave plans, and more. If you’re ready to build for the long term and own the outcome, your journey starts here.</p> <p><strong>Flexible Working&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Samsara, we embrace a flexible working model that caters to the diverse needs of our teams. Our offices are open for those who prefer to work in-person and we also support remote work where it aligns with our operational requirements. For certain positions, being close to one of our offices or within a specific geographic area is important to facilitate collaboration, access to resources, or alignment with our service regions. In these cases, the job description will clearly indicate any working location requirements. Our goal is to ensure that all members of our team can contribute effectively, whether they are working on-site, in a hybrid model, or fully remotely. All offers of employment are contingent upon an individual’s ability to secure and maintain the legal right to work at the company and in the specified work location, if applicable.</span></p> <p><strong>Belonging at Samsara</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we welcome everyone regardless of their background. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, and other characteristics protected by law. We depend on the unique approaches of our team members to help us solve complex problems and want to ensure that Samsara is a place where people from all backgrounds can make an impact.</p> <p><strong>Accommodations&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Samsara is an inclusive work environment, and we are committed to ensuring equal opportunity in employment for qualified persons with disabilities. Please email <a href="mailto:accessibleinterviewing@samsara.com">accessibleinterviewing@samsara.com</a> or <a href="https://forms.gle/DrM6DFQUiSgfPtCVA">click here</a> if you require any reasonable accommodations throughout the recruiting process.</p> <p><strong>Our Commitment to Authenticity</strong></p> <p>We use <a href="https://hiretofu.com/privacyPolicy">Tofu</a>, a fraud detection tool, to validate the authenticity of applications and protect against identity fraud. This ensures we are connecting with real people and allows us to prioritize genuine candidates. Please see Samsara’s <a href="https://www.samsara.com/legal/candidate-privacy-notice">Candidate Privacy Notice</a> for more information.</p> <p><strong>Fraudulent Employment Offers</strong></p> <p>Samsara is aware of scams involving fake job interviews and offers. Please know we do not charge fees to applicants at any stage of the hiring process. Official communication about your application will only come from emails ending in @samsara.com, @us-greenhouse-mail.io or @mail3.guide.co. For more information regarding fraudulent employment offers, please visit our&nbsp;<a href="https://samsara.com/blog/avoiding-fake-job-offers">blog post here</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Renewals Retention Manager (Renewals Strategy Specialist)
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><span style="font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;"><strong>Who we are</strong></span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Samsara (NYSE: IOT) is the pioneer of the Connected Operations™ Cloud, which is a platform that enables organizations that depend on physical operations to harness Internet of Things (IoT) data to develop actionable insights and improve their operations. Representing more than 40% of global GDP, these industries are the infrastructure of our planet, including agriculture, construction, field services, transportation, and manufacturing — and we are excited to help digitally transform their operations at scale.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Working at Samsara means you’ll help define the future of physical operations and be on a team that’s shaping an exciting array of product solutions, including Video-Based Safety, Vehicle Telematics, Apps and Driver Workflows, and Equipment Monitoring. As part of a recently public company, you’ll have the autonomy and support to make an impact as we build for the long term.</span></p></div><h3><strong>About this Role</strong></h3> <p>As the Renewals Retention Manager, you will be the strategic architect behind our retention and growth engine. Reporting to the Senior Manager of Renewals Excellence, you will transform complex datasets into a roadmap for global renewal success. This is not a status update role; you will own the end-to-end renewal analytics and forecasting motions that give our leadership confidence in the pipeline and clarity on churn and contraction drivers.</p> <p>You will be responsible for building the early warning systems and the strategic operational playbooks that allow our field teams to intervene before a customer is downsizing or lost. By partnering closely with Revenue Ops, Sales, Customer Success, and IT, you will ensure our renewal process is data-driven, scalable, and - most importantly - predictable.</p> <p>This role is open to candidates residing in the US except Alaska, Austin Metro, Boulder Metro, California, Chicago Metro, Connecticut, Dallas Metro, Denver Metro, Houston Metro, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York, Rhode Island, Seattle Metro, and Washington, D.C</p> <h3><strong>You should apply if:</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>You are a data storyteller:</strong> You don’t just build dashboards; you extract the reasons to influence executive decision-making.</li> <li><strong>You thrive in the grey area:</strong> You enjoy taking messy, disparate data from Salesforce and SQL and turning it into a streamlined, automated strategy.</li> <li><strong>You are a systems thinker:</strong> You look at a manual process and immediately think about how to automate it through Salesforce or BI tools to drive efficiency.</li> <li><strong>You are proactive, not reactive:</strong> You would rather build a predictive health score today than explain a churn event three months from now.</li> <li><strong>You are a cross-functional collaborator:</strong> You can speak data with IT, strategy with ops, and revenue with sales leaders.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>In this role you will:</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Architect forecasting excellence:</strong> Drive the global renewal forecasting process, integrating customer health insights and predictive analytics to improve accuracy and predictability.</li> <li><strong>Build early warning systems:</strong> Design and maintain sophisticated renewal health scoring models that identify at-risk accounts early enough for successful intervention.</li> <li><strong>Deliver actionable insights:</strong> Analyze churn attribution and expansion patterns to provide Renewals and Customer Success leadership with clear, data-backed action plans.</li> <li><strong>Optimize the renewals engine:</strong> Partner with Revenue Ops and IT to design and implement process automations that reduce manual overhead and improve data integrity.</li> <li><strong>Lead business reviews:</strong> Create and present high-visibility dashboards and quarterly business reviews (QBRs) that communicate performance trends to senior stakeholders.</li> <li><strong>Champion data literacy:</strong> Set the standard for analytical excellence within the Renewals organization, mentoring others on best practices for tools like Salesforce and Tableau.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Minimum Requirements:</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>4+ years of experience</strong> in Renewals, Customer Success Operations, Revenue Operations, or GTM Strategy &amp; Analytics.</li> <li><strong>Advanced Salesforce (SFDC) Expertise:</strong> Deep experience building complex custom report types, dashboards, and calculated fields.</li> <li><strong>Analytical Power-User:</strong> Mastery of SQL and Excel/Google Sheets for complex data modeling, trend analysis, and performance tracking.</li> <li><strong>SaaS Literacy:</strong> Strong understanding of recurring revenue metrics (GRR, NRR, Churn, and Expansion).</li> <li><strong>Communication Skills:</strong> Proven ability to present complex technical findings to non-technical executive audiences.</li> <li><strong>Education:</strong> Bachelor’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Data Science, or a related field.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>An Ideal Candidate also has:</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>BI Tool Mastery:</strong> Hands-on experience building and maintaining data visualizations in platforms like Tableau, Looker, or Sisense.</li> <li><strong>Predictive Analytics Exposure:</strong> Experience using data science principles or statistical modeling to predict customer behavior.</li> <li><strong>Change Management Experience:</strong> A track record of successfully rolling out new tools or workflows to a global, decentralized sales or renewals team.</li> <li><strong>Industry Knowledge:</strong> Experience in high-growth IoT, Fleet Management, or complex Enterprise SaaS environments.</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><span style="font-size: 16px;">The range of annual base salary for full-time employees for this position is below. Please note that base pay offered may vary depending on factors including your city of residence, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Learn more about our total rewards and benefits below.</span></p></div><div class="title">Annual Base Salary</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$70,210</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$94,400 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Total Rewards</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we build for the people who keep the global economy moving. We want owners, not passengers, which is why <a href="https://www.samsara.com/company/careers/benefits">our rewards</a> are designed to fuel high-impact builders. Our compensation program delivers above-market total compensation through a combination of base salary, performance-based bonus/variable pay, and equity (for eligible roles) in a high-growth public company. We meaningfully differentiate pay for our top performers, who have the opportunity to earn above-market compensation that can outpace the broader market over time.</p> <p>Beyond compensation, we provide the foundations that enable long-term success: a flexible, employee-led remote model, a professional development stipend, comprehensive health and parental leave plans, and more. If you’re ready to build for the long term and own the outcome, your journey starts here.</p> <p><strong>Flexible Working&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Samsara, we embrace a flexible working model that caters to the diverse needs of our teams. In these cases, the job description will clearly indicate any working location requirements. All offers of employment are contingent upon an individual’s ability to secure and maintain the legal right to work at the company and in the specified work location, if applicable.</span></p> <p><strong>Belonging at Samsara</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we welcome everyone regardless of their background. We depend on the unique approaches of our team members to help us solve complex problems and want to ensure that Samsara is a place where people from all backgrounds can make an impact.</p> <p><strong>Accommodations&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Samsara is an inclusive work environment, and we are committed to ensuring equal opportunity in employment for qualified persons with disabilities. jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
jobs.description: <p>Our Enterprise Account Executives target and close new business with Datadog’s largest, most strategic customers and prospects. In this role you’ll be focused on uncovering the pain points organizations face as they operate in or migrate to a cloud environment at scale as well as delivering the appropriate Datadog solution.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Prospect into large Fortune 1000 companies in Thailand while running an efficient sales process</li> <li>Maintain, build and own specific relationship maps for your territory including existing relationships and aspirational contacts</li> <li>Develop a deep comprehension of customer's business</li> <li>Negotiate favorable pricing and business terms with large commercial enterprises by selling value and ROI</li> <li>Handle existing customer expectations while expanding reach and depth into assigned territory</li> <li>Demonstrate resourcefulness when faced with challenges that defy easy solution</li> <li>Have intuitive sense of necessary steps to close business and gain customer validation</li> <li>Identify robust set of business drivers behind all opportunities</li> <li>Ensure high forecasting accuracy and consistency</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Someone with 3+ years closing experience (mix of field selling within mid-market or enterprise)</li> <li>Driven and have met/exceeded direct sales goals of 1M+ and operated with an average deal size of $100k+</li> <li>Able to demonstrate methodology to prospect and build pipeline on your own</li> <li>Experienced in working for an innovative tech company (SaaS, IT infrastructure or similar preferred)</li> <li>Experienced in selling into large Fortune 1000 companies with the ability to win new logos</li> <li>Role requires regular travel to client sites, within your area and other regions, using various modes of transportation (car, train, air), depending on business needs.</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>High income earning opportunities based on self performance</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing&nbsp;</li> <li>Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message&nbsp;</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking&nbsp;</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds</li> <li>Generous and competitive medical benefits package</li> <li>Retirement savings match</li> <li>Pet adoption and insurance program</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Remote</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. Together, we champion professional development, diversity of thought, innovation, and work excellence to empower continuous growth. Join the pack and become part of a collaborative, pragmatic, and thoughtful people-first community where we solve tough problems, take smart risks, and celebrate one another. Learn more about #DatadogLife on <a href="https://www.instagram.com/datadoghq/?hl=en" target="_blank">Instagram</a>, <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/datadog" target="_blank">LinkedIn,</a> and <a href="https://learn.datadoghq.com/" target="_blank">Datadog Learning Center. </a></p> <hr> <p data-renderer-start-pos="215"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Equal Opportunity at Datadog:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is proud to offer <a href="https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2023-06/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights6.12ScreenRdr.pdf" target="_blank">equal employment opportunity</a> to everyone regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, veteran status, and other characteristics protected by law. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. Here are our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/candidate-legal-notices/" target="_blank">Candidate Legal Notices</a> for your reference.&nbsp;</p> <p>Datadog endeavors to make our Careers Page accessible to all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our website or need assistance completing the application process, please complete <a href="https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeoJeduEZ2NdZF_65uZmWUrCM-zee7yADXX1s6Xr9zAGq8iKA/viewform" target="_blank">this form</a>. This form is for accommodation requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.&nbsp;</p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662"><strong data-renderer-mark="true">Privacy and AI Guidelines:</strong></p> <p data-renderer-start-pos="662">Any information you submit to Datadog as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Datadog’s&nbsp;<a class="c-link" href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/applicant-candidate-privacy/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">Applicant and Candidate Privacy Notice</a>. For information on our AI policy, please visit <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/legal/interviewing-at-datadog-ai-guidelines/" target="_blank">Interviewing at Datadog AI Guidelines</a>.</p></div>

jobs.description: <p>Our Enterprise Sales Executives target and close new business with Datadog’s largest, most strategic customers and prospects. In this role you’ll be focused on uncovering the pain points organizations face as they operate in or migrate to a cloud environment at scale as well as delivering the appropriate Datadog solution.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.&nbsp;</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Prospect into large Fortune 1000 companies while running an efficient sales process</li> <li>Maintain, build and own specific relationship maps for your territory including existing relationships and aspirational contacts</li> <li>Develop a deep comprehension of customer's business</li> <li>Negotiate favorable pricing and business terms with large commercial enterprises by selling value and ROI</li> <li>Handle existing customer expectations while expanding reach and depth into assigned territory</li> <li>Demonstrate resourcefulness when faced with challenges that defy easy solution</li> <li>Have intuitive sense of necessary steps to close business and gain customer validation</li> <li>Identify robust set of business drivers behind all opportunities</li> <li>Ensure high forecasting accuracy and consistency</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Someone with 3+ years closing experience (mix of field selling within mid-market or enterprise)</li> <li>Driven and have met/exceeded direct sales goals of 1M+ and operated with an average deal size of $100k+</li> <li>Able to demonstrate methodology to prospect and build pipeline on your own</li> <li>Experienced in working for an innovative tech company (SaaS, IT infrastructure or similar preferred)</li> <li>Experienced in selling into large Fortune 1000 companies with the ability to win new logos</li> <li>Role requires regular travel to client sites, within your area and other regions, using various modes of transportation (car, train, air), depending on business needs.</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>High income earning opportunities based on self performance</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSU) and employee stock purchase plan (ESPP)</li> <li>Continuous professional development, product training, and career pathing&nbsp;</li> <li>Sales training in MEDDIC and Command of the Message&nbsp;</li> <li>Intra-departmental mentor and buddy program for in-house networking&nbsp;</li> <li>An inclusive company culture, opportunity to join our Community Guilds</li> <li>Generous and competitive medical benefits package</li> <li>Retirement savings match</li> <li>Pet adoption and insurance program</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Remote</p><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability.
