Please note that hiring for this role is not restricted to the locations listed above.</em></p> <p>The estimated pay ranges for this role are as follows:</p> <ul> <li>Based in Colorado, Hawaii, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Vermont or Washington D.C.: $26.00/hour</li> <li>Based in New York, New Jersey, Washington State, or California (outside of the San Francisco Bay area): $28.00/hour</li> <li>Based in the San Francisco Bay area, California: $31.00/hour</li> </ul> <p>The successful candidate’s starting salary will be determined based on permissible, non-discriminatory factors such as skills, experience, and geographic location.</p> <p>Applications for this role are intended to be accepted until May 1st, but may change based on business needs.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Twilio thinks big. Do you?</strong></p> <p>We like to solve problems, take initiative, pitch in when needed, and are always up for trying new things. That's why we seek out colleagues who embody our values — something we call <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/values">Twilio Magic</a>. Additionally, we empower employees to build <a href="https://twilio.org/support-and-resources/impact-fund">positive change in their communities</a> by supporting their volunteering and donation efforts.</p> <p>So, if you're ready to unleash your full potential, do your best work, and be the best version of yourself, apply now! If this role isn't what you're looking for, <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/jobs#open-positions">please consider other open positions.</a></p> <p><strong>Twilio is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.</strong> We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, reproductive health decisions, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, genetic information, political views or activity, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Additionally, Twilio participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law.</p></div>

jobs.title: Global Security Operations Center Operator
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Who we are&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Twilio, we’re shaping the future of communications, all from the comfort of our homes. We deliver innovative solutions to<a class="c-link" href="https://customers.twilio.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://customers.twilio.com/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">&nbsp;hundreds of thousands of businesses&nbsp;</a>and empower millions of developers worldwide to craft personalized customer experiences.</p> <p>Our dedication to <a class="c-link" href="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" data-sk="tooltip_parent">remote-first work</a>, and strong culture of connection and global inclusion means that no matter your location, you’re part of a vibrant team with diverse experiences making a global impact each day. As we continue to revolutionize how the world interacts, we’re acquiring new skills and experiences that make work feel truly rewarding. Your career at Twilio is in your hands.<br><br>We use Artificial Intelligence (AI) to help make our hiring process efficient. That said, every hiring decision is made by real Twilions!</p> <p><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">.</span></p></div><p><strong>See yourself at Twilio</strong></p> <p>Join the team as Twilio’s next Operator, Global Security Operations Center (GSOC).&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>About the job</strong></p> <p>This position is needed to maintain a broad situational awareness over internal and external physical events that could impact employee safety or pose a threat to business assets or operations across Twilio’s global footprint. GSOC Operators monitor a variety of data sources including internal access control systems, external incident aggregators, travel safety applications, and general open source reporting materials. GSOC Operators serve as the primary contact for employees with physical safety and security questions or concerns. When an incident occurs, the GSOC Operator may dispatch security resources, escalate to crisis management teams, send broad communications to Twilio employees, or execute a mass notification to account for employee safety.</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities:</strong></p> <p>In this role, you’ll:</p> <p><strong>Monitoring and Surveillance</strong></p> <ul> <li>Utilize various physical security asset monitoring and surveillance systems to detect potential unauthorized access,&nbsp; suspicious activity, or other security incidents</li> <li>Monitor open-source threat intelligence and other internal SaaS tools to maintain situational awareness of persistent or emergency global events with potential or direct impact to operations</li> </ul> <p><strong>Security Operations Support</strong></p> <ul> <li>Support access control and visitor management processes</li> <li>Manage security-related calls for service and provide general guidance to employees, vendors, and guests</li> <li>Maintain awareness of business travelers and personnel accessing Twilio facilities</li> <li>Partner with local site security teams to escalate and dispatch resources for incident and alarm response</li> <li>Review travel requests to elevated risk locations, assist travelers through the approval process, and cross-reference live itineraries with external incidents&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Incident Management&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Perform the first point of incident intake across a variety of emergencies, including medical emergencies, natural disasters, fire/evacuation, travel disruptions, and security incidents</li> <li>Assess and triage events and incidents to determine their impact to operations</li> <li>Dispatch and coordinate response with emergency response personnel&nbsp;</li> <li>Communicate effectively on emerging incidents to large audiences to share situational awareness or tactical updates as warranted</li> <li>Maintain real-time incident documentation for reporting and analysis</li> <li>Perform Life-Safety checks for Twilio in areas impacted by potentially life-threatening conditions</li> <li>Participate in post-incident reviews to document and implement corrective action and improvements to future response</li> </ul> <p><strong>Operational Excellence</strong></p> <ul> <li>Maintain a strong understanding of security and emergency standard operating procedures</li> <li>Utilize critical thinking and problem solving skills to analyse situations and implement solutions</li> <li>Support large projects that will improve Twilio’s GSOC capabilities and initiatives&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Qualifications&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Twilio values diverse experiences from all kinds of industries, and we encourage everyone who meets the required qualifications to apply. If your career is just starting or hasn't followed a traditional path, don't let that stop you from considering Twilio. We are always looking for people who will bring something new to the table!</p> <p><strong>*Required:</strong></p> <ul> <li>3+ years of experience in a GSOC working environment</li> <li>Experience with physical safety and security technology, systems (ACS, IDS, VSS, VMS), travel safety, and mass notification tools</li> <li>Experience with open-source intelligence (OSINT) research tools&nbsp;</li> <li>Strong critical thinking and problem-solving skills</li> <li>Proven ability to follow standard operating procedures and playbooks</li> <li>Ability to prioritize tasks in a fast-paced environment</li> <li>Ability to stay calm, professional, and think clearly in high-stress situations</li> <li>Ability to communicate clearly and concisely; verbal and written</li> <li>Ability to work ‘non-standard’ shift hours, to overlap as needed with colleagues and stakeholders in other global locations, including weekend and holiday hours</li> <li>English Language Proficiency</li> </ul> <p><strong>Desired:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Customer service mindset</li> <li>Comfortable with a high-tech work environment&nbsp;</li> <li>Eager to learn new tools and processes</li> <li>Ability to work independently and as part of a team</li> <li>Proficiency in Google Suite</li> <li>Additional Language Proficiency</li> </ul> <p><strong>Shift Details:</strong><strong><br></strong><strong>Variable (8–10-hour shifts)</strong></p> <p><strong>Must be flexible to work holidays, weekends, and occasional overtime.</strong></p> <p><strong>Location</strong></p> <p>This role will be remote, but is not eligible to be hired in CA, CT, NJ, NY, PA, WA.</p> <p><strong>Travel&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>We prioritize connection and opportunities to build relationships with our customers and each other. For this role, approximately 10% travel is anticipated to help you participate in project or team in-person meetings to help you connect in a meaningful way.</p> <p><strong>What We Offer</strong></p> <p>Working at Twilio offers many benefits, including competitive pay, generous time off, ample parental and wellness leave, healthcare, a retirement savings program, and much more. Offerings vary by location.</p> <p><strong>Compensation</strong></p> <p><em>*Please note this role is open to candidates outside of California, Colorado, Hawaii, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, Minnesota, New Jersey, New York, Vermont, Washington D.C., and Washington State. The information below is provided for candidates hired in those locations only.</em></p> <p>The estimated pay ranges for this role are as follows:</p> <ul> <li>Based in Colorado, Hawaii, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Vermont or Washington D.C. : $26.27 - $32.84</li> <li>Based in New York, New Jersey, Washington State, or California (outside of the San Francisco Bay area): $27.77 - $34.71</li> <li>Based in the San Francisco Bay area, California: $30.85 - $38.56</li> <li>This role may be eligible to participate in Twilio’s equity plan and corporate bonus plan. All roles are generally eligible for the following benefits: health care insurance, 401(k) retirement account, paid sick time, paid personal time off, paid parental leave.</li> </ul> <p>The successful candidate’s starting salary will be determined based on permissible, non-discriminatory factors such as skills, experience, and geographic location.</p> <p>Applications for this role are intended to be accepted until May 15 but may change based on business needs.&nbsp;</p> <p><br><br></p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Twilio thinks big. Do you?</strong></p> <p>We like to solve problems, take initiative, pitch in when needed, and are always up for trying new things. That's why we seek out colleagues who embody our values — something we call <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/values">Twilio Magic</a>. Additionally, we empower employees to build <a href="https://twilio.org/support-and-resources/impact-fund">positive change in their communities</a> by supporting their volunteering and donation efforts.</p> <p>So, if you're ready to unleash your full potential, do your best work, and be the best version of yourself, apply now! If this role isn't what you're looking for, <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/jobs#open-positions">please consider other open positions.</a></p> <p><strong>Twilio is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.</strong> We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, reproductive health decisions, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, genetic information, political views or activity, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Additionally, Twilio participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law.</p></div>

jobs.title: Graphic Design Intern (June 22nd start, 12 weeks)
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Who we are&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Twilio, we’re shaping the future of communications, all from the comfort of our homes. We deliver innovative solutions to<a class="c-link" href="https://customers.twilio.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://customers.twilio.com/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">&nbsp;hundreds of thousands of businesses&nbsp;</a>and empower millions of developers worldwide to craft personalized customer experiences.</p> <p>Our dedication to <a class="c-link" href="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" data-sk="tooltip_parent">remote-first work</a>, and strong culture of connection and global inclusion means that no matter your location, you’re part of a vibrant team with diverse experiences making a global impact each day. As we continue to revolutionize how the world interacts, we’re acquiring new skills and experiences that make work feel truly rewarding. Your career at Twilio is in your hands.<br><br>We use Artificial Intelligence (AI) to help make our hiring process efficient. That said, every hiring decision is made by real Twilions!</p> <p><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">.</span></p></div><h3><strong>About the job</strong></h3> <p>We are looking for an enthusiastic <strong>Graphic Design Intern</strong> to join our Brand Team. This role is designed for a developing creative who is eager to learn the ropes of a world-class brand, working closely with senior designers and art directors to bring the Twilio story to life across various touchpoints.</p> <p>As a Graphic Design Intern at Twilio, you won’t just be watching from the sidelines, you’ll be an active contributor to our creative output. You will support the Brand Team by executing design tasks that range from social media assets and presentation decks to event signage and internal communications. This is a highly collaborative role where you will receive mentorship and feedback to help refine your craft in a fast-paced, global environment.</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Support &amp; execute:</strong> Assist Senior Designers and Art Directors in the production of high-quality visual assets for digital, social, and print platforms.</li> <li><strong>Maintain brand consistency:</strong> Learn and apply Twilio’s brand guidelines rigorously, ensuring every piece of work, no matter how small, aligns with our visual identity.</li> <li><strong>Iterate &amp; refine:</strong> Take direction and feedback from creative leads to iterate on designs, helping to move projects from concept to final delivery.</li> <li><strong>Production assistance:</strong> Prepare files for production, including resizing assets for various channels, styling presentations, and organizing creative libraries.</li> <li><strong>Collaborate:</strong> Participate in creative brainstorms and team meetings, bringing fresh perspectives and a "builder" mindset to the table.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Qualifications</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Actively working</strong> on or have a Bachelor level degree in Graphic Design or 1-3 years of work experience in a design-related field</li> <li><strong>Foundational skills:</strong> A strong understanding of typography, layout, color theory, and composition.</li> <li><strong>Technical proficiency:</strong> Early mastery of Figma and Adobe Creative Cloud (Illustrator, Photoshop, InDesign)</li> <li><strong>Attention to detail:</strong> An eagle eye for alignment, spelling, and brand accuracy.</li> <li><strong>Growth mindset:</strong> A hunger to learn, a positive attitude toward feedback, and the ability to manage multiple small tasks simultaneously.</li> <li><strong>Portfolio:</strong> A link to your portfolio or work samples that demonstrate your design thinking and execution (to submit on application).&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>and art directors to bring the Twilio story to life across various touchpoints.</p> <p>As a Graphic Design Intern at Twilio, you won’t just be watching from the sidelines, you’ll be an active contributor to our creative output. You will support the Brand Team by executing design tasks that range from social media assets and presentation decks to event signage and internal communications. This is a highly collaborative role where you will receive mentorship and feedback to help refine your craft in a fast-paced, global environment.</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Support &amp; execute:</strong> Assist Senior Designers and Art Directors in the production of high-quality visual assets for digital, social, and print platforms.</li> <li><strong>Maintain brand consistency:</strong> Learn and apply Twilio’s brand guidelines rigorously, ensuring every piece of work, no matter how small, aligns with our visual identity.</li> <li><strong>Iterate &amp; refine:</strong> Take direction and feedback from creative leads to iterate on designs, helping to move projects from concept to final delivery.</li> <li><strong>Production assistance:</strong> Prepare files for production, including resizing assets for various channels, styling presentations, and organizing creative libraries.</li> <li><strong>Collaborate:</strong> Participate in creative brainstorms and team meetings, bringing fresh perspectives and a "builder" mindset to the table.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Location<br></strong><span style="font-size: 14px;"><br>This role will be remote, based in the US.&nbsp;</span></h3> <h3><strong>What we offer</strong></h3> <p>There are many benefits to working at Twilio, including, in addition to competitive pay, things like generous time-off, ample parental and wellness leave, healthcare, a retirement savings program, and much more. Offerings vary by location.</p> <p><strong>Compensation</strong></p> <p><strong><br></strong><em>*Please note the salary range information provided applies only to candidates residing in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, Minnesota, New Jersey, New York, Vermont, Washington D.C., and Washington State due to local requirements. Compensation for candidates in other locations will be discussed during the hiring process. Please note that hiring for this role is not restricted to the locations listed above.</em></p> <p>The estimated pay ranges for this role are as follows:</p> <ul class="ul1"> <li class="li1">Based in Colorado, Hawaii, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Vermont, or Washington D.C.: $27.00/hourly</li> <li class="li1">Based in New York, New Jersey, Washington State, or California (outside of the San Francisco Bay area): $28.00/hourly</li> <li class="li2">The successful candidate’s starting salary will be determined based on permissible, non-discriminatory factors such as skills, experience, and geographic location within the state.</li> </ul> <p class="p1"><span class="s1">&nbsp;• </span>Applications for the role will be accepted on an ongoing basis until May 1st.<span class="Apple-converted-space">&nbsp;</span></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Twilio thinks big. Do you?</strong></p> <p>We like to solve problems, take initiative, pitch in when needed, and are always up for trying new things. That's why we seek out colleagues who embody our values — something we call <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/values">Twilio Magic</a>. Additionally, we empower employees to build <a href="https://twilio.org/support-and-resources/impact-fund">positive change in their communities</a> by supporting their volunteering and donation efforts.</p> <p>So, if you're ready to unleash your full potential, do your best work, and be the best version of yourself, apply now! If this role isn't what you're looking for, <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/jobs#open-positions">please consider other open positions.</a></p> <p><strong>Twilio is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.</strong> We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, reproductive health decisions, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, genetic information, political views or activity, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Additionally, Twilio participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law.</p></div>

jobs.title: Deal Pricing
jobs.description: <h1><strong>About Stripe</strong></h1> <p>Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career.</p> <h3><strong>About the Team</strong></h3> <p>Deal Pricing is a dedicated global function that provides deal pricing expertise, financial insights and deal support for negotiated users to Stripe’s global Go-to-Market teams. We work to ensure deals create economic value for Stripe by establishing the deal commercial framework for a scaling sales organization, actively supporting large and strategic deals and providing pricing insights and feedback to sales, finance and product leaders. Deal Pricing frequently works with cross-functional business partners across Operations, Finance &amp; Strategy and Product and there is constant collaboration with Deal Pricing colleagues across all regions. AMER Deal Pricing covers teams in the United States, Canada and Latin America and are located in Stripe offices and remote locations across the region.</p> <h2><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></h2> <p>You will be instrumental in providing thought leadership to regional sales teams and senior executives and continuing to develop our framework, approach and associated processes in order to achieve mutually beneficial deals with our sold Users while mitigating risk to Stripe. In addition to providing key financial insights and recommendations to business partners and Stripe leadership, you will have a chance to stretch beyond the boundaries of finance through business case development and commercial negotiations with all types of customers across the full suite of Stripe’s financial and payment products.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Serve as a key financial and strategic thought partner to specific AMER Go-to-Market sales teams and their leadership in both pricing deals at scale and pricing strategic deals&nbsp;</li> <li>Be responsible for defining and implementing our pricing and margin guidance and identifying levers for financial uplift</li> <li>Develop and refine new frameworks to evaluate a deal’s attractiveness to Stripe, ensuring alignment between these frameworks, the company’s long range plan and mutually agreeable outcomes with cross-functional business partners</li> <li>Maintain up to date pricing benchmarks and contribute to team analytical design and production efforts, using this data to enhance our pricing philosophy</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Who You Are</strong></h2> <p>We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement.</p> <h3><strong>Minimum Requirements</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Minimum 3+ years of experience in finance or deal pricing at a company, or experience within investment banking or, management consulting</li> <li>A proven ability to build collaborative working relationships with business partners through a combination of influencing and awareness</li> <li>The ability to deal effectively with ambiguity and thrive in an unstructured, fast-moving environment by driving business results without significant supervision</li> <li>Excellent financial modeling, verbal and written communication skills</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Preferred Qualifications</strong></h3> <p><em>Experience in the payments or technology sector</em></p> <ul> <li>Experience presenting findings and recommendations to senior leadership, preferably to sales of go-to-market business partners</li> <li>Experience with SQL and Tableau or a willingness to learn is a must</li> </ul>

jobs.title: Engineering Manager, HADR
jobs.description: <p><strong>Who we are</strong></p> <h3><strong>About Stripe</strong></h3> <p>Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career.</p> <h3><strong>About the team</strong></h3> <p>In this role, you will be joining the High Availability and Disaster Recovery team. At Stripe, availability is a core feature of our products. This team designs and builds new solutions to allow latency-critical, stateful applications to survive any type of disaster. We build distributed systems on top of unreliable architecture to provide highly available and resilient customer solutions. This team is creating greenfield solutions which will serve as the basis for Stripe’s architecture 5, 10, or 20 years into the future.</p> <p>This is a distributed team with many remote engineers. You are encouraged to apply if you meet the minimum requirements and are able to work from anywhere in the United States or Canada.</p> <h2><strong>What you’ll do</strong></h2> <p>You will help develop our global architecture by combining less-available components and data centers into a highly available and resilient whole. You will work on latency-critical solutions where every millisecond matters and data redundancy is a hard requirement. You will learn quickly and work on a broad range of problems - one day may be investigating Mongo write concerns, the next may be minimizing cross-region TLS handshakes, followed by developing new systems to automate disaster detection and failovers. Your work will enable Stripe to increase the GDP of the internet by providing uptime and data protection which have historically been impossible.</p> <h3><strong>Responsibilities</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Lead and manage a team of talented engineers on the team, providing mentorship, guidance, and support to ensure their success.</li> <li>Drive the execution of projects, overseeing the entire development lifecycle from planning to delivery, while maintaining high standards of quality and timely completion.</li> <li>Help influence peers / managers and build consensus while dealing with ambiguity</li> <li>Build your team - formalizing role definitions, defining charter and ownership boundaries and taking a newly formed team into a high-functioning one&nbsp;</li> </ul> <h2><strong>Who you are</strong><strong><em>&nbsp;</em></strong></h2> <p>We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement.</p> <h3><strong>Minimum requirements</strong></h3> <p>This is where you’ll include the minimum requirements for the job. These are the absolute minimum experiences and skills needed to be considered for the position. Any candidate, whether outbound, inbound, or referred, who does not meet these, will not be considered so be fastidious when listing these.</p> <ul> <li>4+ years of software development experience&nbsp;</li> <li>2+ years of cloud development or management experience</li> <li>Professional working proficiency in English</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Preferred qualifications</strong></h3> <ul> <li>Understanding of distributed system concepts (ex. leader election, voting, quorum)</li> <li>Background in high-availability systems, chaos engineering, or disaster recovery design</li> <li>Experience with cloud infrastructure and multi-region deployments</li> <li>Familiarity with document databases such as MongoDB</li> </ul>

jobs.title: Cashiering Support Associate
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><h2>Join us in building the future of finance.</h2> <p>Our mission is to democratize finance for all. <a href="https://www.cerulli.com/press-releases/cerulli-anticipates-124-trillion-in-wealth-will-transfer-through-2048" target="_blank">An estimated $124 trillion of assets</a> will be inherited by younger generations in the next two decades. The largest transfer of wealth in human history. If you’re ready to be at the epicenter of this historic cultural and financial shift, keep reading.</p></div><h2><strong>About the team + role</strong></h2> <p>We are building an elite team, applying frontier technologies to the world’s biggest financial problems. We’re looking for bold thinkers. Sharp problem-solvers. Builders who are wired to make an impact. Robinhood isn’t a place for complacency, it’s where ambitious people do the best work of their careers. We’re a high-performing, fast-moving team with ethics at the center of everything we do. Expectations are high, and so are the rewards.&nbsp;</p> <p>The Cashiering team’s mission is to ensure accurate, timely movement of funds across customer brokerage and retirement accounts while delivering a compliant, high-quality client experience.&nbsp;As a Cashiering Specialist, you will own high-volume cashiering workflows—from ACH and wires to checks and transfers—serving as a key operational link between advisors, clients, custodians, and internal partners.</p> <p><strong>This role is based in our Chicago, IL, Clearwater, FL, or Westlake, TX office(s), with in-person attendance expected at least 3 days per week.</strong></p> <p>At Robinhood, we believe in the power of in-person work to accelerate progress, spark innovation, and strengthen community. Our office experience is intentional, energizing, and designed to fully support high-performing teams.&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><strong>What you’ll do</strong></h2> <ul> <li>You process a high volume of cashiering transactions (ACH, wires, checks, journals, custodian-to-custodian transfers) accurately, efficiently, and in line with internal policies and regulatory requirements.</li> <li>You monitor and manage daily transaction queues to meet strict deadlines and service-level expectations, researching and resolving discrepancies and inquiries end-to-end.</li> <li>You serve as a primary contact for advisors, their staff, and clients across phone, email, and internal systems, providing clear updates and a seamless, professional experience.</li> <li>You will partner with our clearing firm and internal teams (Operations, Compliance, Technology, Advisor Services) to facilitate transactions, resolve issues, and support cross-functional initiatives.</li> <li>You contribute to continuous improvement by identifying opportunities to enhance efficiency, accuracy, and client experience within cashiering operations.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What you bring</strong></h2> <ul> <li>You have strong knowledge of cashiering procedures, workflows, and regulatory considerations, along with a basic understanding of financial markets and the securities industry.</li> <li>You are exceptionally detail-oriented and organized, able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, high-volume, deadline-driven environment.</li> <li>You communicate clearly and professionally, with excellent data entry, written, and verbal skills, and you’re comfortable engaging with clients and advisors over the phone.</li> <li>You bring prior back-office operations experience—preferably in financial services or banking—and are confident handling high-volume transactional workflows using tools like Microsoft Word and Excel.</li> <li>You are a proactive problem-solver with strong analytical, decision-making, and critical thinking skills, who takes ownership, adapts quickly to new systems and technologies, and follows issues through to resolution.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What we offer</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Challenging, high-impact work to grow your career.</li> <li>Performance-driven compensation with multipliers for outsized impact, bonus programs, equity ownership, and 401(k) matching.</li> <li>Best-in-class benefits to fuel your work, including 100% paid health insurance for employees with 90% coverage for dependents.</li> <li>Lifestyle wallet — a highly flexible benefits spending account for wellness, learning, and more.</li> <li>Employer-paid life &amp; disability insurance, fertility benefits, and mental health benefits.</li> <li>Time off to recharge including company holidays, paid time off, sick time, parental leave, and more!</li> <li>Exceptional office experience with catered meals, events, and comfortable workspaces.</li> </ul><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><strong>In addition to the base pay range listed below, this role is also eligible for bonus opportunities + equity + benefits.</strong></p> <p>Base pay for the successful applicant will depend on a variety of job-related factors, which may include education, training, experience, location, business needs, or market demands. The expected base pay range for this role is based on the location where the work will be performed and is aligned to one of 3 compensation zones. For other locations not listed, compensation can be discussed with your recruiter during the interview process.</p> <p><span style="text-decoration: underline;">Base Pay Range:</span></p></div><div class="title">Zone 1 (Menlo Park, CA; New York, NY; Bellevue, WA; Washington, DC)</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$20.40</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$24 USD</span></div></div><div class="pay-input"><div class="title">Zone 2 (Denver, CO; Westlake, TX; Chicago, IL)</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$20.40</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$24 USD</span></div></div><div class="pay-input"><div class="title">Zone 3 (Lake Mary, FL; Clearwater, FL; Gainesville, FL)</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$19.60</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$23 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p>Click <a href="https://careers.robinhood.com/benefits" target="_blank">here</a> to learn more about our Total Rewards, which vary by region and entity.</p> <p>If our mission energizes you and you’re ready to build the future of finance, we look forward to seeing your application.</p> <p>Robinhood provides equal opportunity for all applicants, offers reasonable accommodations upon request, and complies with applicable equal employment and privacy laws. Inclusion is built into how we hire and work—welcoming different backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences so everyone can do their best. Please review the&nbsp;<a href="https://careers.robinhood.com/applicantprivacypolicy" target="_blank">Privacy Policy</a> for your country of application.</p></div>

jobs.title: Compliance Operations Specialist 1
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Who we are&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Twilio, we’re shaping the future of communications, all from the comfort of our homes. We deliver innovative solutions to<a class="c-link" href="https://customers.twilio.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://customers.twilio.com/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">&nbsp;hundreds of thousands of businesses&nbsp;</a>and empower millions of developers worldwide to craft personalized customer experiences.</p> <p>Our dedication to <a class="c-link" href="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" data-sk="tooltip_parent">remote-first work</a>, and strong culture of connection and global inclusion means that no matter your location, you’re part of a vibrant team with diverse experiences making a global impact each day. As we continue to revolutionize how the world interacts, we’re acquiring new skills and experiences that make work feel truly rewarding. Your career at Twilio is in your hands.<br><br>We use Artificial Intelligence (AI) to help make our hiring process efficient. That said, every hiring decision is made by real Twilions!</p> <p><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">.</span></p></div><p><strong>See yourself at Twilio</strong></p> <p>Join the team as Twilio’s next Compliance Operations Specialist</p> <p><strong>About the job</strong></p> <p>This role is part of Twilio’s Compliance Operations team, which helps ensure that customer activity on our platform follows industry rules and Twilio’s internal policies. You’ll be responsible for reviewing alerts and account activity for Messaging and Voice traffic to assess whether it complies with Twilio’s Voice Policy, Messaging Policy, Acceptable Use Policy, and relevant regulatory requirements across global carrier networks.</p> <p>When non-compliant activity is identified, such as messaging that violates local telecom rules or improper use of voice services, you’ll work with customers to explain the issue and guide them toward a compliant solution. In some cases, you’ll also take enforcement action to stop the non-compliant activity from continuing on Twilio’s platform.</p> <p>This is an analytical and communication-heavy role that requires strong judgment, attention to detail, and the ability to work through ambiguous situations. You’ll be expected to make informed decisions and explain those decisions clearly to customers, even when the guidance may not always be black and white.</p> <p>We’re looking for someone who enjoys digging into complex issues, learning how global telecom regulations apply to real-world traffic, and helping customers understand how to stay compliant. The ideal candidate is curious, investigative by nature, and comfortable with both independent research and customer-facing communication.</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <p>In this role, you’ll:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Investigate potential violations:</strong> Review account activity, messaging behavior, and data to identify signs of abuse or misuse. You'll be trained to spot red flags and patterns linked to spam or scams, and take quick action to mitigate risk.</li> <li><strong>Stop bad actors before they cause harm:</strong> You'll use internal tools and available data to find and shut down non-compliant activity that could negatively impact Twilio’s customers or consumers who receive unwanted calls or texts.</li> <li><strong>Communicate directly with customers:</strong> A major part of the job is customer-facing, primarily through email via our ticketing platform Zendesk. You'll notify customers of policy violations, provide them with clear guidance on how to fix issues, and sometimes inform them that their access to Twilio is being restricted or terminated.</li> <li><strong>Analyze trends and prevent future abuse:</strong> Go beyond individual cases by recognizing recurring themes, identifying root causes, and helping recommend long-term solutions to keep bad actors off the platform.</li> <li><strong>Work with data:</strong> You’ll regularly examine call and messaging records to trace activity, identify patterns, and support enforcement decisions with evidence.</li> <li><strong>Support global impact:</strong> The work you do directly affects businesses, mobile carriers, and end users around the world. Your decisions may impact high-profile brands and millions of consumers globally.</li> <li><strong>Prioritize clarity and accuracy:</strong> Because most communication is written, especially through email, you’ll need strong writing skills to explain policy violations and technical details in a way that customers understand and can act on.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Qualifications&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Twilio values diverse experiences from all kinds of industries, and we encourage everyone who meets the required qualifications to apply. If your career is just starting or hasn't followed a traditional path, don't let that stop you from considering Twilio. We are always looking for people who will bring something new to the table!</p> <p><strong>*Required:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Willingness to work a non-traditional schedule; weekends, holidays and evenings may be required</li> <li>Experience working in a fast paced, ambiguous environment, with a strong bias for action</li> <li>You’re empathetic and love working with customers and carriers to solve their problems and questions, backed with data.&nbsp;</li> <li>You are detail-oriented with good organizational skills and comfortable with ambiguity.</li> <li>You have above average written and verbal communication skills and are able to articulate concepts/ideas in a clear, concise manner.&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>You are willing to collaborate with communications partners and players in the industry to resolve issues, troubleshoot, and build trust.</li> <li>You have the ability to make sound decisions in a fast paced environment.&nbsp;</li> <li>You have good time management and organizational skills and are comfortable working under pressure.&nbsp;</li> <li>Basic math skills.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Desired:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Basic knowledge of using spreadsheets including formulas.</li> <li>Have 1-2 years experience in fraud, abuse or compliance in ecommerce, FinTech, or telecommunications.</li> <li>1-2 years experience working in a queue based environment triaging and resolving tickets.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Location</strong></p> <p>This role will be based remotely in Colombia.</p> <p><strong>Travel&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>We prioritize connection and opportunities to build relationships with our customers and each other. For this role, you may be required to travel occasionally to participate in project or team in-person meetings.</p> <p><strong>What We Offer</strong></p> <p>Working at Twilio offers many benefits, including competitive pay, generous time off, ample parental and wellness leave, healthcare, a retirement savings program, and much more. Offerings vary by location.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Twilio thinks big. Do you?</strong></p> <p>We like to solve problems, take initiative, pitch in when needed, and are always up for trying new things. That's why we seek out colleagues who embody our values — something we call <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/values">Twilio Magic</a>. Additionally, we empower employees to build <a href="https://twilio.org/support-and-resources/impact-fund">positive change in their communities</a> by supporting their volunteering and donation efforts.</p> <p>So, if you're ready to unleash your full potential, do your best work, and be the best version of yourself, apply now! If this role isn't what you're looking for, <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/jobs#open-positions">please consider other open positions.</a></p> <p><strong>Twilio is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.</strong> We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, reproductive health decisions, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, genetic information, political views or activity, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Who we are&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Twilio, we’re shaping the future of communications, all from the comfort of our homes. We deliver innovative solutions to<a class="c-link" href="https://customers.twilio.com/" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://customers.twilio.com/" data-sk="tooltip_parent">&nbsp;hundreds of thousands of businesses&nbsp;</a>and empower millions of developers worldwide to craft personalized customer experiences.</p> <p>Our dedication to <a class="c-link" href="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" target="_blank" data-stringify-link="https://www.twilio.com/en-us/blog/open-work" data-sk="tooltip_parent">remote-first work</a>, and strong culture of connection and global inclusion means that no matter your location, you’re part of a vibrant team with diverse experiences making a global impact each day. Your career at Twilio is in your hands.<br><br>We use Artificial Intelligence (AI) to help make our hiring process efficient. That said, every hiring decision is made by real Twilions!</p> <p><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">.</span></p></div><p><strong>See yourself at Twilio</strong></p> <p>Join the team as Twilio’s next Senior Field Enablement Business Partner</p> <p><strong>About the job</strong></p> <p>This person in this position will report directly to the Senior Manager, GTM Sales Enablement working closely with their peers in the GTM Enablement organization as we continue to transform the business and state of Enablement. If you like adding value and seeing the direct impact of your work, this role is for you. If you thrive in highly dynamic environments and love rolling your sleeves up to get things done, again,this role is for you. &nbsp;</p> <p>As a Field Enablement Business Partner, you will play a strategic role in aligning enablement initiatives with business objectives, acting as the connective tissue between SDR (Sales Development) leadership, marketing, product, and operations. You will be responsible for designing, executing, and optimizing enablement programs that empower SDRs to perform at their best, shorten ramp times, improve conversion rates, and drive revenue growth. You’ll partner closely with sales leaders to identify skill gaps, define key competencies, and deliver tailored enablement solutions.</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <p>In this role, you’ll:</p> <ul> <li>Act as a consultant for sales leaders on enablement opportunities based on both qualitative and quantitative metrics and performance</li> <li>Deliver content tailored by audience&nbsp;</li> <li>Contribute to knowledge bases, training sessions, knowledge development, and weekly updates&nbsp;</li> <li>Proactively engage with the leadership team to plan, prioritize and execute on the enablement plan&nbsp;</li> <li>Provide feedback on the effectiveness of curriculums. Propose adjustments or new curriculum additions&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> <li>Partner with the Learning Design Experience team to develop learning objectives and measurable outcomes for all enablement programs in your area</li> <li>Follow through on the adoption of pre / post- evaluations and metrics of enablement programs&nbsp;</li> <li>Maintain knowledge of Twilio’s competitor’s products, company direction, financial stability, etc&nbsp;&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Qualifications&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Twilio values diverse experiences from all kinds of industries, and we encourage everyone who meets the required qualifications to apply. If your career is just starting or hasn't followed a traditional path, don't let that stop you from considering Twilio. We are always looking for people who will bring something new to the table!</p> <p><strong>*Required:</strong></p> <ul> <li>1-3+ years in customer value, sales enablement, or direct sales experience, with at least 2years in a strategic enablement/business partner role.</li> <li>Proven success in driving measurable impact through enablement programs.</li> <li>Strong understanding of the B2B sales cycle, particularly in SaaS or complex sales environments.</li> <li>Experience with sales enablement platforms (e.g., Highspot, Salesloft, Gong), LMS, and CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce).</li> <li>Excellent communication, facilitation, and stakeholder management skills.</li> <li>Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret data and make informed decisions.</li> <li>Self-starter with the ability to manage multiple initiatives in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.</li> <li>Understanding of sales processes, gained from having worked closely with sales teams, or from having been a sales professional.&nbsp;</li> <li>Team player who recognizes team contributions and celebrates team wins, who is open to new ideas, and comes with a positive attitude, a sense of urgency, and willingness to try what is needed to achieve project goals.&nbsp;</li> <li>Demonstrates different influence styles as appropriate to each situation while maintaining positive relationships.&nbsp;</li> <li>Builds and maintain trusting relationships with peers and stakeholders</li> <li>Knowledge of current and emerging adult learning theory, and how to put it into practice when designing learning initiatives.&nbsp;</li> <li>Ability to leverage Bloom’s Taxonomy to establish learning outcomes and results</li> </ul> <p><strong>Location</strong></p> <p>This role will be remote, and based in Canada.</p> <p><strong>Travel&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>We prioritize connection and opportunities to build relationships with our customers and each other. For this role, approximately 15-20% travel is anticipated to help you connect in-person in a meaningful way.&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What We Offer</strong></p> <p>Working at Twilio offers many benefits, including competitive pay, generous time off, ample parental and wellness leave, healthcare, a retirement savings program, and much more. Offerings vary by location.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Twilio thinks big. Do you?</strong></p> <p>We like to solve problems, take initiative, pitch in when needed, and are always up for trying new things. That's why we seek out colleagues who embody our values — something we call <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/values">Twilio Magic</a>. Additionally, we empower employees to build <a href="https://twilio.org/support-and-resources/impact-fund">positive change in their communities</a> by supporting their volunteering and donation efforts.</p> <p>So, if you're ready to unleash your full potential, do your best work, and be the best version of yourself, apply now! If this role isn't what you're looking for, <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/jobs#open-positions">please consider other open positions.</a></p> <p><strong>Twilio is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.</strong> We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, reproductive health decisions, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, genetic information, political views or activity, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p><strong>Who we are&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>At Twilio, we’re shaping the future of communications, all from the comfort of our homes. That said, every hiring decision is made by real Twilions!</p> <p><span style="color: rgb(255, 255, 255);">.</span></p></div><p><strong>See yourself at Twilio</strong></p> <p>Join the team as Twilio’s next MDM Data Specialist</p> <p><strong>About the job</strong></p> <p>We are looking for a detail-oriented Data Steward to own the integrity of our enterprise data. In this role, you will be the frontline defender of Data Quality, implementing rigorous monitoring and remediation processes to eliminate silos. You will manage the lifecycle of our master data, ensuring compliance with Data Governance policies while working directly with technical teams to optimize our MDM hubs. If you have a passion for precision and thrive on organizing complex information, you’ll fit right in.</p> <p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p> <p>In this role, you’ll:</p> <ul> <li>Manage and maintain the "Golden Record" for core data domains (Customer and Product), including resolving data conflicts and duplicates.</li> <li>Manage data hierarchies, relationships, and cross-system mapping.</li> <li>Oversee the matching and merging process to improve consolidation of data.</li> <li>Establish, measure and monitor Data Quality KPIs (Accuracy, Completeness, Timeliness, Consistency)</li> <li>Perform root-cause analysis on data discrepancies and lead remediation efforts.</li> <li>Design and implement automated data profiling, validation and cleansing processes.</li> <li>Support the development of data governance standards, policies, and business rules.</li> <li>Document data lineage, business glossaries, and data dictionaries.</li> <li>Facilitate data stewardship activities across business units</li> </ul> <p><strong>Qualifications&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Twilio values diverse experiences from all kinds of industries, and we encourage everyone who meets the required qualifications to apply. If your career is just starting or hasn't followed a traditional path, don't let that stop you from considering Twilio. We are always looking for people who will bring something new to the table!</p> <p><strong>*Required:</strong></p> <ul> <li>3-5 years of experience in data management, data stewardship, or related roles.</li> <li>Strong SQL skills for data profiling and analysis.</li> <li>Strong understanding of Master Data Management concepts and best practices</li> <li>Experience with data quality tools and methodologies</li> <li>Familiarity with data visualization tools to report on data quality metrics.</li> <li>Proficient understanding of data lifecycle management and data modeling concepts.</li> <li>Excellent communication skills in English; the ability to explain technical data issues to non-technical stakeholders.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Desired:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Familiarity with MDM platforms (Informatica, Reltio, Stibo, etc.)</li> <li>Foundation in Data Governance methodologies and foundations</li> <li>Experience with Cloud Data Warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, or Databricks).</li> <li>Knowledge of industry-specific data standards.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Location</strong></p> <p>This role will be remote, and based in Colombia.</p> <p><strong>Travel&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>We prioritize connection and opportunities to build relationships with our customers and each other. For this role, you may be required to travel occasionally to participate in project or team in-person meetings.</p> <p><strong>What We Offer</strong></p> <p>Working at Twilio offers many benefits, including competitive pay, generous time off, ample parental and wellness leave, healthcare, a retirement savings program, and much more. Offerings vary by location.</p><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Twilio thinks big. Do you?</strong></p> <p>We like to solve problems, take initiative, pitch in when needed, and are always up for trying new things. That's why we seek out colleagues who embody our values — something we call <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/values">Twilio Magic</a>. Additionally, we empower employees to build <a href="https://twilio.org/support-and-resources/impact-fund">positive change in their communities</a> by supporting their volunteering and donation efforts.</p> <p>So, if you're ready to unleash your full potential, do your best work, and be the best version of yourself, apply now! If this role isn't what you're looking for, <a href="https://www.twilio.com/company/jobs#open-positions">please consider other open positions.</a></p> <p><strong>Twilio is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.</strong> We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, reproductive health decisions, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, genetic information, political views or activity, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Additionally, Twilio participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law.</p></div>
